**Subject:** Year-End 2000 Performance Review – Action Required  

Dear Reviewer,  

You have been selected to participate in Enron’s Year-End 2000 Performance Management Process by providing constructive feedback for designated employees. Your insights are invaluable to this initiative and directly contribute to achieving our organizational goals.  

To submit your feedback:  
1. Log in to the **Performance Evaluation Portal (PEP)** at [http://pep.corp.enron.com](http://pep.corp.enron.com).  
2. Navigate to **Performance Review Services** and select **"Perform Review."**  

**Key Deadline:**  
All feedback forms must be completed by **Friday, November 17, 2000**.  

**Support Resources:**  
For assistance, please contact the PEP Help Desk:  
- **Houston:** +1.713.853.4777 (Option 4)  
- **London:** +44.207.783.4040 (Option 4)  
- **Email:** [perfmgmt@enron.com](mailto:perfmgmt@enron.com)  

Thank you for your commitment to this critical process. Your participation ensures a fair and comprehensive evaluation for our teams.  

Best regards,  
Enron Performance Management Team

"I must preface by stating that I did not purchase EU when it initially launched, and I acquired it on sale, which included EU and XCOM2. This allowed me to avoid the issues that some players have reported experiencing with the game. I thoroughly enjoyed EU, despite it being unforgiving in the early stages, as it provided a sense of accomplishment when I executed tasks correctly. In contrast, XCOM2 has been a different story. I've found it to be a well-optimized game that runs smoothly on my mid-range hardware, a GTX560 with 2GB of VRAM. Initially, I set the graphics settings to high, but it did experience some lag, although it was not to the point of being unplayable. However, when I adjusted the settings to medium, the game ran without any issues. It's possible that I've been fortunate when it comes to encountering problems with games, or perhaps it's just a matter of perspective, but it seems that many of the negative reviews are coming from players who appear to be overly critical and entitled.

Prince, the 'Top Music Earner' of 2004, surpassed all other pop stars, including Madonna and Elton John, according to Rolling Stone's annual list. The singer's lucrative musicology tour and album sales earned him a staggering $56.5 million, a sum that far exceeded that of his closest competitor, Madonna, who earned $54.9 million from her global re-invention tour. Simon and Garfunkel, veterans of the music industry, took 10th place, with their comeback tour generating $24.9 million in revenue. According to Rolling Stone, Prince's return to the spotlight after a decade-long hiatus was a major comeback story. His 2004 tour was a resounding success, raking in a staggering $90.3 million in ticket sales, with his album, Musicology, selling a remarkable 1.9 million copies.

Mps tout lords replacement plan a group of mps has tried to raise the pressure on tony blair over reform to the house of lords by publishing a detailed blueprint for change. The cross-party group has unveiled a draft bill proposing a smaller second chamber in which 70% of members would be elected. Mps and peers have failed to agree on reform since 1999 when 600 hereditaries lost their seats. The group says it can win support for removing the last 92 hereditaries. The government postponed plans to remove the remaining hereditary peers because they said they were unlikely to succeed after opposition in the lords. Tony blair has argued. There needs to be consensus on reforms. There have been suggestions there will be proposals for changing at least the powers of the lords in labour's manifesto.

I'm excited to finally start my blog, but I'm still unsure what it will look like. As I spend most of my time in front of a computer, I might as well make the most of my time and share my thoughts on various topics that have been swirling around in my head. I was inspired by a blogger who kept me up to date with her daily life, sharing her experiences in a relatable and entertaining manner.

My journey to understanding the significance of birth certificates in international development began 20 years ago, when my family was in the process of adopting my two younger brothers from Ethiopia. A seemingly innocuous question posed by my mother sparked a profound realization: "What date should we use as their birthday?" My initial response, "the day they were born, of course," was met with a sobering reality check. My mother explained that neither of my brothers possessed a birth certificate, rendering their exact birthdates unknown. This revelation had a profound impact on me, and it has since evolved into a lifelong pursuit to address the global issue of missing birth certificates. Today, I am driven to explore the critical role that birth certificates play in international development, and to find innovative solutions to this complex problem.

The so-called "spam king" of the internet, Scott Richter, is now seeking bankruptcy protection as anti-spam laws take their toll. Richter, who runs OptInRealBig.com and has sent billions of spam emails, claims that ongoing legal challenges have driven the company to file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. One of the primary legal battles OptInRealBig is facing is with Microsoft, which is demanding millions of dollars in damages. The company believes that filing for Chapter 11 will allow it to address its legal challenges while continuing its business operations. Ranked as the world's third-largest spammer by the junk mail monitoring group Spamhaus, OptInRealBig was sued in December 2003 for distributing emails that breached anti-spam regulations. The lawsuit was initiated by Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer, who accuse Richter and his cohorts of sending billions of spam emails through 514 compromised internet addresses across 35 countries.

Customer purchasing decisions are significantly influenced by online product reviews, yet this system remains vulnerable to manipulation through fabricated evaluations—commonly known as "review spam." Professional spammers continually refine their tactics, developing increasingly sophisticated methods to bypass detection systems. These deceptive strategies present substantial challenges  they evolve rapidly over time, vary between individual spammers and targeted products, and most critically, often remain entirely undetected. Current spam detection approaches primarily emphasize accuracy metrics, which fails to adequately address the broader objective of preserving review integrity. To overcome these limitations, we propose a minimax game framework that pits spammers against detection systems in a strategic competition based on practical objectives beyond mere detection accuracy. This approach yields Nash equilibrium solutions, producing robust detectors capable of countering diverse spamming strategies while maintaining system stability.

The phrase, 'to do two things at once is to do neither,' serves as a compelling critique of multitasking. It's often linked to the Roman writer Publilius Syrus, though, as is common with such quotes, the attribution is uncertain. My curiosity, however, lies in the truth of this adage. While it clearly applies to scenarios like emailing during dinner, texting while driving, or perhaps live tweeting during a TED Talk, I believe there are certain activities where multitasking—or even aiming to accomplish three or four tasks simultaneously—is beneficial. Consider Albert Einstein in 1905, when he published four groundbreaking scientific papers.

The proposals outlining policing plans for local communities would require residents to elect their own area police commissioners, under plans unveiled by the Conservative Party. Party leader Michael Howard stated that the new role would replace "inefficient" police authorities. He clarified that the new office would not supersede the role of a Chief Constable. The Liberal Democrats, however, expressed concerns that the plan could lead to extreme groups dominating policing, while Labour criticized the "extravagant" Conservative promises on policing. Responding to the plans, the Chairman of the Police Federation of England and Wales, which represents rank-and-file officers, emphasized the importance of retaining operational independence. Jan Berry, the Chairman, warned  "It is a service, not a political football to be kicked around every time an election approaches. These plans could result in those with extreme political views dictating what actually happens on the ground.

Suddenly, over the past two days, I've been overwhelmed with intense exhaustion—utterly debilitating fatigue. Last evening, I went home and immediately fell asleep, only waking when my mother called at around eight-thirty. When I awoke, the room was completely dark, as I hadn’t turned on any lights. For a few seconds, I was disoriented about the time and date, just my mother's voice guiding me back to reality. I wonder why I’m feeling so drained... The weather is pleasant this morning. It was drizzling as I arrived. Meanwhile, I've heard that in Calcutta, it has been raining for the last three days.

Cardinal criticises iraq war cost billions of pounds spent on conflict in iraq and in the middle east should have been used to reduce poverty, cardinal cormac murphy-o'connor has said. The head of the catholic church in england and wales made the comments on bbc radio 4 and will re-iterate his stance in his christmas midnight mass. The cardinal used a christmas message to denounce the war in iraq as a "terrible" waste of money. He and the archbishop of canterbury have both spoken out about the war. Speaking on bbc radio 4's thought for the day slot, he criticised the fact that "billions" have been spent on war, instead of being used to bring people "out of dire poverty and malnourishment and disease". The cardinal said 2005 should be the year for campaigning to "make history poverty".

Like a lot of people around the world, earlier this summer my friends and i were obsessed with the women's world cup held in france. Here we are, watching these incredible athletes, the goals were amazing, the games were clean and engaging, and at the same time, outside the field, these women are talking about equal pay, and in the case of some countries, any pay at all for their sport. So because we were mildly obsessed, we wanted to watch the games live, and we decided that one of the spanish-speaking networks in the us was the best place for us to start. And it wasn't until a few games into the tournament that a friend of mine talks to me and says, "why does it feel like everything i'm seeing is commercials for makeup and household cleaning products and diets?

Sid Meier's Civilization: Beyond Earth is a turn-based game that takes the series to new heights by setting the game in the future and introducing new mechanics such as the affinity system and orbital layer. While I enjoy the new features, I found the game to be overwhelming at times due to the sheer amount of new mechanics to learn. One area where the game could be improved is by incorporating new artwork and visuals, particularly in the communication mechanics with other leaders. This would help to enhance the overall immersive experience and make the game more engaging.

This groundbreaking paper presents a cutting-edge millimeter-scale CMOS 64x64 single charged particle radiation detector system for external beam cancer radiotherapy. By leveraging a 1x1 μm^2 diode for energy deposition measurement in the depletion region, the innovative array design boasts a colossal detection area of 512x512 μm^2. Instead of relying on voltage drop sensing, the proposed system revolutionizes the field by measuring the pulse width, or the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline, providing a more accurate and efficient means of radiation detection.

The prototype ASIC, fabricated in TSMC's 65 nm LP CMOS process, consumes an average static power of just 0.535 mW under 1.2 V analog and digital power supply, making it a power-efficient and compact solution. To further validate the system's functionality, the authors successfully verified its operation in a clinical 67.5 MeV proton beam setting, marking a significant breakthrough in the field of radiation detection. With its unparalleled detection capabilities and energy efficiency, this system poises to transform the field of cancer radiotherapy and beyond.

We developed single-shot shaped pulses for ultra high fidelity (uh-fidelity) population transfer on a 3-level quantum system in lambda configuration. To ensure high fidelity, we use the lewis-riesenfeld (l-r) method to derive a family of solutions leading to an exact transfer, where the solutions follow a single dynamical mode of the l-r invariant. Among this family, we identify a tracking solution with a single parameter to control simultaneously the fidelity of the transfer, the population of the excited state, and robustness. We define a measure of the robustness of an uh-fidelity transfer as the minimum percentile deviation on the pulse areas at which the infidelity rises above $10^{-4}$. The robustness of our shaped pulses is found superior to that of gaussian and adiabatically-optimized pulses for moderate pulse areas.

Things are quite hectic here at the moment, but I always find time for stress-relieving activities. I'm not very skilled at 8-ball or ping pong, and it shows - Rick (the Canuck) consistently beats me. We have a ping pong table in the front lobby of our building, so I try to squeeze in a quick game whenever I can. Last night, I participated in an 8-ball tournament at the recreation tent, complete with a popcorn machine, multiple TVs, a jukebox, DVD rentals, a pool table, a dartboard, and, of course, a ping pong table. Despite my lack of skill, I was doing well in my game until I scratched on the eight ball. However, I still had a good time, and I was even joined by two friendly Dutch soldiers who decided to join in on the tournament.

Arnesen denies rift with santini tottenham sporting director frank arnesen has denied that coach jacques santini resigned because of a clash of personalities at white hart lane. There had been newspaper speculation that santini had felt undermined by arnesen's role at the club. "it is absolutely not true," arnesen told bbc radio five live. "there is only one thing that made him resign and that is his own personal problems. "he has talked to me recently and said this matter is absolutely for himself." arnesen said he was unable to throw any light onto the problems that caused santini to quit after just 13 games in charge. He added: "jacques has never gone into exactly what it was. But i trust him in that; you have to accept it. I think we should respect it.

In this work, a generalization of the study of the human gait was made from already existent models in the literature, like models of keller and kockshenev. In this hybrid model, a strategy of metabolic energy minimization is combined in a race process, with a non-linear description of the movement of the mass center's libration, trying to reproduce the behavior of the walk-run transition. The results of the experimental data, for different speed regimes, indicate that the perimeter of the trajectory of the mass center is a relevant quantity in the quantification of this dynamic. An experimental procedure was put into practice in collaboration with the research group in biomedical engineering, basic sciences and laboratories of the manuela beltr\'an university in bogot\'a, colombia.

Saturday we took connor to firemans park, thinking that the fireman's easter egg hunt was to start at 11am. It turned out it was on the friday. Thank goodness i brought a bag of eggs with me just incase. So connor had a great time finding eggs out in the park. Sunday morning when he woke up he was so excited that the easter bunny had come. There is nothing better then to see the excitement on your childs face. After egg hunting, we all went to church. Even though the 'easter bunny' is fun and all. We do make sure our kids know the true meaning of the holidays. This was megan's first easter, she looked so cute dressed in her jean dress and stockings :)

Even nature's most disgusting creatures have important secrets, but who would want a swarm of cockroaches coming towards them?yet one of the greatest differences between natural and human technologies relates to robustness. Robust systems are stable in complex and new environments. Remarkably, cockroaches can self-stabilize running over rough terrain. When we put a jet pack on them, or give them a perturbation like an earthquake, we discovered that their wonderfully tuned legs allow them to self-stabilize without using any of their brainpower. They can go over complex terrain like grass, no problem, and not get destabilized. We discovered a new behavior where, because of their shape, they actually roll automatically to their side to go through this artificial test bit of grass.robust systems can perform multiple tasks with the same structure. Here's a new behavior we've discovered.

Processing spontaneous conversations in real-world environments, like those found in child-centered recordings, is recognized as one of the most complex audio challenges. Successfully developing speech processing models capable of handling such a diverse range of conditions would greatly benefit language acquisition research, which focuses on the quantity and quality of speech children are exposed to and produce. Additionally, these models would be invaluable for early diagnosis and evaluating the impact of interventions. In this paper, we introduce our methodology for creating an open-source neural network designed to categorize audio segments into vocalizations by the child wearing the recording device, vocalizations from other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To achieve this, we compiled a diverse set of child-centered corpora, amassing a total of 260 hours of recordings spanning 10 languages.

Metal ion implantation into ceramics has been demonstrated to be an effective and controllable technique for tailoring the surface electrical conductivity of the ceramic piece, and this approach has been used in a number of applications. Importantly, it provides a method for grading the voltage drop across high voltage insulators, and thereby increasing the maximum operational voltage that can be applied across the insulator without surface flashover. However a concern for the use of the method is the long term stability of the implantation-induced conductivity, this especially so if the implanted metal species is readily oxidized. Here we report on our examination of the long-term behavior of the surface conductivity of titanium-implanted alumina. The results indicate that after an initial drop of as much as 40% in the first few weeks after implantation, the conductivity shows only a very slow decrease of about 10% over the following year.

The aftermath of returning from practicum proved challenging, with the first week being particularly difficult. However, by the second week, clarity began to emerge. What became evident is that post-practicum trauma is a widespread yet overlooked issue only now gaining recognition.  

Initially overwhelmed, I found relief after meeting with the course coordinator, where I shared my concerns. This not only lightened my burden but also helped prevent another student from facing the same pedagogically inadequate practicum environment.  

Just yesterday, I encountered a distressed classmate who was adamant about warning the next student assigned to her problematic school. I reassured her and suggested we first address the issue with the administration rather than acting impulsively.

Undertale comes together to make something greater than the sum of its parts. The graphics are solid for a retro title and the music is nice, but paired together they make some truly beautiful and impacting moments. The script runs the gammut from comedic to dramatic to heartbreaking to hopeful, sometimes all at once, and does this while turning rpg tropes on their head. And all of this without touching on the world, characters, story, gameplay... I could write whole pages about it. But instead, i'll just say this: if you like rpgs, play this game. If you like video games, play this game. If you have seven free hours between now and your death, play this game.

My heinous mood has passed. Forthcoming period + forgetting to take prozac until late in the day = very sad, prone to tears kristin. But surprise of boyfriend at my doorstep + ice cream sundae + holding boyfriend's hand after i had a terribly scary dream in which people started shooting machine guns at the news bureau = kristin feeling better. And i don't have to work this morning la la la la la but i do have to call a man at kmiz to get his rate card. And tonight i get to see two of my best girls, amy and kate (hopefully with time to study in between). And tomorrow maybe i will see someone i haven't seen for quite awhile for our ww date.

**Subject:** Exciting Sales Director Opportunities with DTE Energy  

Dear Kim,  

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Joelene Whittaker, and I’m a recruiter with STM Associates. We’ve been engaged by DTE Energy (www.dtecs.com/sitemap.html) to identify two highly qualified **Directors of Sales** for their Eastern and Western regions.  

**Position Details:**  
- **Eastern Region Director:** Based in Ann Arbor, Michigan  
- **Western Region Director:** Based in Denver, Colorado  
Both roles will report directly to the Vice President of Sales.  

**Ideal Candidate Profile:**  
- Minimum of **5 years of sales experience**, with at least **2 years in a trading company** (preferred).  
- Deep expertise in the **U.S. coal, transportation, and electric utility sectors**, including coal tolling, marketing, and origination functions supporting coal trading operations.  

If this aligns with your background or network, I’d love to discuss further. Let me know your availability for a quick call.  

Best regards,  
Joelene Whittaker  
STM Associates

We report on the thermally induced transverse vibrations of a photonic crystal waveguide (PCW). The waveguide is composed of two parallel nanobeams separated by a 240 nm vacuum gap. We have developed and validated models to translate the motion of these beams into phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe traveling through a guided mode (GM) in the waveguide. This analysis is applicable for probe frequencies both far from and near the dielectric band edge. The PCW was specifically designed for near-field atom trapping, and this study lays the groundwork for assessing potential adverse effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps located close to the waveguide surfaces. Our long-term objectives include creating robust atom-mediated interactions between individual vibration phonons and single photons traveling in the PCW’s guided modes, thus paving the way for quantum-level opto-mechanical systems integrating atoms, photons, and phonons.

Blue beat u2 to top france honour irish band u2 have been honoured at france's biggest music awards, but were beaten to a prize by boy band blue. U2 received a special achievement prize at the nrj music awards, but blue beat them to the international group award. Us band maroon 5 was named best new international artist, and took the best international song title for this love. More than five million radio listeners voted in the awards. The international male and female prizes went to usher and avril lavigne respectively. Collecting his band's award from model naomi campbell at the cannes ceremony, u2 frontman bono said in french: "i'm not from this country but i'll make a little confession to you - it's at the cote d'azur i feel at home."

The spreadsheet below, provided by Jdasovic.nsf, outlines the implicit commodity charges based on the effective date of four different rates within the same rate schedule (Schedule DR). The schedule in effect from August 2000 to September 2000 results in a significantly higher commodity charge due to a lump sum reimbursement that SDG&E customers receive over those two months, leading to lower expected electric bills. If the lump sum reimbursement is excluded from the implicit energy component, the average from June 2000 to January 2001 is 6.2 cents, whereas the average implicit commodity from February 2001 to December 2001 is 7.3 cents. Overall, the average implicit commodity charge from June 2000 to December 2001 is 6.9 cents per unit. For deliveries starting on September 1, 2000, the average remains 6.9 cents.

I have come to the realization that my days of monopoly are behind me, and I have vowed to never again place my hands on that particular game piece. Tonight, I was defeated by a small margin and won the Farqu game, possibly due to my opponent's departure after a few short hours and my desire to ensure he felt good on his final night here. Despite the outcome, I must admit that the story cannot compare to the Texas-sized headache I am currently experiencing. I have taken some Aleve, and I must say that it is doing the trick. All I need now is some well-deserved rest and relaxation. Your telephone is the one that helped me wrap the cord around your neck, which I found particularly frustrating at the drive-in.

Shah Rukh Khan's mantra of success  courage, unwavering determination, and an unrelenting vision. These are the hallmarks of the most accomplished individuals, whose unyielding spirits refuse to accept defeat. To them, seemingly insurmountable challenges are merely a canvas waiting to be conquered. Our next speaker, Bhakti Sharma, embodies this unshakeable resolve, as she navigates the vast expanse of long-distance swimming with ease. Ladies and gentlemen, please join me in welcoming the fearless Bhakti Sharma. (applause)

Bhakti Sharma  Imagine a two-and-a-half-year-old child, carefree and full of wonder, riding pillion on a moped behind her mother on a sweltering summer afternoon in Rajasthan, with no notion of the journey's destination. And just 20 minutes later, that same child finds herself fully immersed in the cool waters of a pool, as if she had always been meant to be there.

As I gaze into the mirror today, I behold a multifaceted individual  a justice and education scholar at Columbia University, a youth mentor, an activist, and a future New York State Senator. (Cheering) I see a man who, despite spending a quarter of his life behind bars, has emerged from a tumultuous past. Six years of imprisonment, beginning at the tender age of a teenager on Rikers Island, nearly cost a man his life. However, it was not the punishment I endured as a teenager in adult prison or the harsh realities of our legal system that transformed me. Instead, it was the transformative power of a classroom that revealed to me, and indeed to our justice system as a whole, the possibility of redemption. Just a few weeks prior to my release on parole, a counselor encouraged me to enroll in a groundbreaking college course being offered within the prison walls, an opportunity that proved to be a turning point in my journey towards a brighter future.

**Britain's Economic Future Takes Center Stage in Election Campaign**  

Chancellor Gordon Brown has declared that the nation's economic prospects will form the cornerstone of Labour's election strategy. His remarks followed the final cabinet meeting at 10 Downing Street ahead of the anticipated election announcement. Brown emphasized that voters would acknowledge Labour's record of delivering economic stability and sustained growth—a commitment the party pledges to uphold.  

Meanwhile, the Conservatives unveiled initiatives to address antisocial behavior, while the Liberal Democrats expanded on their plans to reform local taxation.  

In a notable intervention, the Archbishop of Canterbury issued an open letter to all major political parties, urging them to refrain from exploiting public anxieties during the campaign. He implored leaders to avoid reducing the election to a contest of fearmongering over issues such as terrorism, immigration, and crime.

The past weekend was a respite of unbridled indulgence, marked by a long-overdue reunion with the soothing allure of nature and the sonic landscapes of Nevermore, a band whose discography I had inexplicably neglected for far too long. This period of relaxation also afforded me the opportunity to devour a substantial amount of literature, culminating in the completion of three novels: Bruce Sterling's 'Heavy Weather', Larry Niven's 'World of Ptavvs', and Octavia Butler's 'Parable of the Sower'. Of these, 'Heavy Weather' proved to be a captivating cyberpunk escapade, set against the backdrop of a dystopian near-future United States ravaged by the devastating consequences of global warming and societal chaos. While Sterling's masterful storytelling and meticulous world-building rendered the narrative both convincing and engaging, I couldn't help but feel that the experience, though enjoyable, was somewhat lacking in depth and emotional resonance. The characters, though skillfully crafted and vividly realized, failed to leave an indelible mark on my psyche, leaving me to conclude that, although 'Heavy Weather' is undoubtedly a well-crafted novel, it falls short of being truly exceptional.

**UK Conservatives Propose Mandatory Health Screenings for Immigrants**  

The UK's opposition Conservative Party has announced plans to implement comprehensive health checks for immigrants should they win the upcoming general election. Key details of the proposed policy include:  

- **Targeted Screening:** Individuals relocating to the UK from outside the European Union for work or residence will be subject to medical evaluations. Those arriving from high-risk tuberculosis (TB) regions intending to stay six months or longer must undergo mandatory chest X-rays, with additional tests if necessary.  
- **Extended Stay Requirement:** All non-EU nationals applying to reside in the UK for a year or more will be required to complete a full medical examination.  
- **Focus on Infectious Diseases:** Screenings will prioritize detecting TB, hepatitis B, and HIV. A positive TB diagnosis will result in automatic visa rejection, while other medical conditions will be assessed individually.  

This initiative aims to safeguard public health while ensuring a structured approach to immigration policy.

City's 0-2 defeat to Manchester United on Sunday has left them nine points behind the leaders in the Premier League, after a scrappy victory for the Red Devils. Wayne Rooney scored the opener with a low shot that deflected off Richard Dunne and into the net, before Steve McManaman wasted a glorious chance to level the score with a shot from three yards out in the first half. Manchester United's approach play was less fluid than patient in the opening 45 minutes, but they managed to fashion just one chance - a Paul Scholes header over the bar. The victory for Manchester United was a significant blow to City's title hopes, as they now trail the leaders by nine points with just 11 games remaining in the season.

This game was a surprising gem. One often sees rave reviews of certain products that are often misleading. In this case, the reviews reflect the fun of this neat little game. It is an odd mix between a classic 2d platformer and an moba, and does it with style. I found myself enjoying characters that i wouldn't enjoy in other moba games. I had expected this trait to continue, but decided to give a melee tank a go anyway. I was pleasantly surprised with the outcome and ended up trying out more as they were unlocked from playing games. The art style and interface is oddly charming. As such, i now use one of the characters as my avatar and a wallpaper from creating a card set for the game.

Coz' this is the aftermath the first week back from practicum was a struggle but it is only in the second week that the smoke has really started to clear. Post-practicum trauma was a hidden epidemic that is only now coming to light. A highly anticipated documentary film featuring the life and career of chart-topping singer George Michael will be showcased at this year's Berlin Film Festival. The documentary, set to screen in the Panorama section of the festival, which runs from February 10-20, offers a candid look at the highs and lows of Michael's life, from his early days in Wham! to his more recent experiences. The 93-minute film explores his journey from his early days as a member of Wham! to his later years as a solo artist, including his legal battles with Sony Records and his stance on the Iraq War and American politics. Michael will attend the festival to introduce the screening on February 16, alongside Director Southan Morris and Executive Producer Andy Stephens. This is a unique opportunity to gain insight into the life and career of a music legend, and the Berlin Film Festival is honored to present this special event.

The nonlinear fluorescence emission has been widely applied for the high spatial resolution optical imaging. Here, we studied the fluorescence anomalous saturating effect of the nitrogen vacancy defect in diamond. The fluorescence reduction was observed with high power laser excitation. It increased the nonlinearity of the fluorescence emission, and changed the spatial frequency distribution of the fluorescence image. We used a differential excitation protocol to extract the high spatial frequency information. By modulating the excitation laser's power, the spatial resolution of imaging was improved approximate 1.6 times in comparison with the confocal microscopy. Due to the simplicity of the experimental setup and data processing, we expect this method can be used for improving the spatial resolution of sensing and biological labeling with the defects in solids.

In the framework of general relativity, the Kerr spacetime stands as the sole stationary solution describing an isolated black hole, characterized by a distinctive multipolar structure determined exclusively by its mass and angular momentum. This study introduces a comprehensive methodology for computing multipole moments in generic stationary spacetimes, which we subsequently apply to an extensive class of horizonless microstate geometries. Unlike the Kerr metric, these solutions may violate both axial and equatorial symmetries, exhibiting a far more intricate multipolar structure that offers a phenomenological window into constraining fuzzball models. Our numerical analysis reveals a consistent trend: all multipole moments in these geometries generally exceed (in absolute magnitude) their Kerr black hole counterparts with equivalent mass and spin parameters. While present observational constraints on black-hole candidates' quadrupole moments impose only modest limitations on fuzzball scenarios, forthcoming gravitational-wave observations from LISA's extreme mass-ratio inspirals are projected to enhance these constraints by several orders of magnitude.

New drink limit 'would cut toll' more lives than previously thought could be saved by cutting drink-drive limits by a third, a report says. University college london research claims cutting the limit from 80mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood to 50mg would save 65 lives a year. The findings are being published by the parliamentary advisory council for transport safety as mps are due to vote on the government's road safety bill. The bill includes changes to speeding fines but not to the drink-drive limit. The research, carried out by transport expert professor richard allsop, says cutting the limit would also lead to 230 fewer serious injuries on the uk's roads compared with 2003. He estimates that this would save the country £119m a year. A previous estimate, contained in a 1998 department of transport document, suggested such a move could save 50 lives a year.

The paper proposes a new framework called Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNN) that leverages temporal point processes and variational autoencoders to learn temporal attention between nodes in a graph. The proposed framework aims to alleviate issues associated with human-specified edges, which can be both expensive to produce and suboptimal for downstream tasks.
 The key idea is to learn temporal attention between nodes by observing node interactions over time. This is achieved by using temporal point processes to model the dynamics of node interactions and variational autoencoders to infer temporal attention between nodes.
The proposed framework consists of two main components:
1. Temporal Point Processes (TPPs): TPPs are used to model the dynamics of node interactions over time. TPPs are based on the idea of a point process, where each node in the graph is represented by a point in a high-dimensional space. The position of each point in this space captures the state of the node at a given time step.
2. Variational Autoencoders (VAEs): VAEs are used to infer temporal attention between nodes. VAEs are based on the idea of variational inference, which is a method for approximating complex probability distributions. In the context of TGNNs, VAEs are

Ireland delivered a commanding performance to overcome Scotland at Murrayfield, keeping their Six Nations Grand Slam aspirations firmly alive. Despite an early 8-0 lead for the Scots, courtesy of Hugo Southwell's try, Ireland seized control before halftime with tries from locks Malcolm O'Kelly and Paul O'Connell. The visitors further extended their dominance after the break, as winger Denis Hickie crossed for a third try while Ronan O'Gara contributed 13 points with his boot. Though Jon Petrie touched down for Scotland's second try, late scores from John Hayes and Gavin Duffy ensured Ireland's triumph.  

With two hard-fought away victories secured, Eddie O'Sullivan's squad now turns its attention to hosting England at Lansdowne Road in two weeks' time. Meanwhile, Scotland, still searching for their first Six Nations win under coach Matt Williams, face another challenge when Italy visit Edinburgh—their inability to convert pressure into points remaining a persistent concern.

I recall that when I was 14, my family embarked on an extraordinary journey to adopt my younger siblings from Ethiopia. One day, my mother posed a thought-provoking question  'What day should we commemorate for their birthday?' My initial response was, 'the day they were born, of course!' However, my mother's follow-up inquiry, 'Well, Kristen, neither of your little brothers have a birth certificate, so how do you suggest we determine the correct date?' left me stunned. Two decades later, I am still grappling with this enigmatic puzzle, only now I channel my efforts towards a more profound issue  the significance of birth certificates in the context of international development. What does this seemingly mundane document have to do with the complexities of global development?

Downloads enter us singles chart digital music downloads are being included in the main us singles chart for the first time. Billboard's hot 100 chart now incorporates data from sales of music downloads, previously only assigned to a separate download chart. Green day's boulevard of broken dreams is currently number two in billboard's pop chart, and tops its digital chart. Download sales are due to be incorporated into the uk singles chart later this year. Digital sales in the us are already used to compile billboard's hot digital sales chart. They will now be tallied with sales of physical singles and airplay information to make up its new hot 100 chart. Its second new chart - the pop 100 - also combines airplay, digital and physical sales but confines its airplay information to us radio stations which play chart music.

This text introduces novel concepts and perspectives, as well as challenges, in measuring and controlling open quantum systems through sequential methods. It explores how protocols that utilize both repeated quantum measurements and dynamical decoupling control pulses can: (i) safeguard and confine quantum dynamics against decoherence in alignment with Zeno physics; (ii) provide an analytical prediction for the likelihood of transferring a quantum system into a targeted quantum state via stochastic sequential measurements; and (iii) optimally reconstruct the spectral density of environmental noise by orthogonalizing filter functions in the frequency domain for a designed quantum sensor. Accomplishing these objectives will significantly enhance our ability to observe and manipulate open quantum systems, leading to progress in quantum science and technology.

Online reviews play a crucial role in helping customers make informed purchasing decisions. Unfortunately, these evaluations can be compromised by sophisticated spammers who have developed increasingly sophisticated and adaptable strategies to evade detection. The ever-evolving nature of spamming tactics makes it challenging to capture and counter them effectively. Moreover, existing detectors often prioritize detection accuracy over the preservation of the trustworthiness of product evaluations, which can lead to a mismatch between their goals. To address this issue, we propose a novel approach that pits spammers against spam detectors in a game-like environment, where their respective goals are not solely focused on detection accuracy. By achieving a Nash equilibrium in this game, we can develop stable detectors that are resilient to any mixed detection strategies, thereby maintaining the integrity of online reviews and promoting a trustworthy online marketplace.

When a new user signs up on a website, we often lack any prior information about them. This means they have no history of interacting with items, no established user profile, and no connections with other users on the platform. Despite this, it is essential that our recommender systems engage new users from the outset, enticing them to remain on the site and become active participants. This challenge is known as the new user cold-start problem, and it is critical to both the growth and even the viability of a company. Current solutions for addressing user cold-start scenarios typically involve asking for additional input from users, such as through an interview process, or leveraging supplementary data like user demographics, location, or social networks [10]. Nevertheless, users might be reluctant to participate in interviews, and often, this supplementary information is not readily available for new users.

Two-dimensional materials with low symmetry, such as ReS₂ and ReSe₂ monolayers, black phosphorus monolayers, group-IV monochalcogenide monolayers, and borophene, exhibit more intricate atomic structures compared to the simple honeycomb lattices found in graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. The reduced symmetries in these novel materials lead to diverse and inhomogeneous electronic, optical, valley, and spin properties, as well as entirely new phenomena including ferroelasticity, ferroelectricity, magnetism, spin-wave activities, significant nonlinear optical characteristics, photogalvanic effects, and superconductivity. Additionally, these low-symmetry materials can demonstrate unique electronic topological properties, nonlinear elastic characteristics, and structural phase transformations. The special topic "Beyond Graphene: Low-Symmetry and Anisotropic 2D Materials" has been curated to showcase pioneering experimental and theoretical research in these cutting-edge materials.

We report observations of thermally driven transverse vibrations of a photonic crystal waveguide (PCW), with a particular focus on their impact on optical atomic traps near the surfaces of the PCW. The PCW consists of two parallel nanobeams with a 240 nm vacuum gap between them, and we develop and validate models for the transduction of beam motion to phase and amplitude modulation of a weak optical probe propagating in a guided mode (GM) of the PCW. Our findings provide a foundation for evaluating the potential deleterious effects of thermal motion on optical atomic traps, with long-term goals of achieving strong atom-mediated links between individual phonons of vibration and single photons propagating in the GMs of the PCW. This would enable opto-mechanics at the quantum level with atoms, photons, and phonons."

Do you recall the legendary Age of Mythology? It's been an eternity since we've seen a real-time strategy game of that caliber. Divinity: Dragon Commander comes remarkably close to claiming the title of my all-time favorite RTS. What truly sets it apart is its ambitious fusion with a sweeping, Risk-inspired grand strategy layer—a combination that delivers perfection. While I'll admit the game's dragon transformation mechanic (allowing aerial support, direct assaults, and real-time battlefield command) might influence my enthusiasm, I strive to maintain objectivity. Modern RTS titles like Civilization V certainly have their merits with intricate systems and layered mechanics. Yet they often overwhelm players by cramming convoluted systems, endless resource charts, and excessive complexity into a single experience.

Entanglement is a fundamental component in quantum information processing, and its detection is crucial for harnessing its power. A widely employed method for identifying entanglement is through the use of entanglement witnesses, which are operators that yield non-negative measurement outcomes for all separable states, while potentially producing negative results for certain entangled states. However, constructing an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled systems poses significant challenges due to the exponential growth of the Hilbert space dimension with respect to system size. In contrast, for a specific class of n-partite Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ)-like states, a robust witness scheme has been developed, requiring n+1 local measurement settings and capable of withstanding up to 50% white noise contamination. Notably, a more efficient witness for GHZ-like states has also been proposed, necessitating only two local measurement settings and tolerating up to 33% white noise, thereby offering a more practical solution for entanglement detection in these systems.

Low-symmetry 2d materials---such as res$_2$ and rese$_2$ monolayers, black phosphorus monolayers, group-iv monochalcogenide monolayers, borophene, among others---have more complex atomistic structures than the honeycomb lattices of graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. The reduced symmetries of these emerging materials give rise to inhomogeneous electron, optical, valley, and spin responses, as well as entirely new properties such as ferroelasticity, ferroelectricity, magnetism, spin-wave phenomena, large nonlinear optical properties, photogalvanic effects, and superconductivity. Novel electronic topological properties, nonlinear elastic properties, and structural phase transformations can also take place due to low symmetry. The "beyond graphene: low-symmetry and anisotropic 2d materials" special topic was assembled to highlight recent experimental and theoretical research on these emerging materials.

The pursuit of causal insights from observational data is a cornerstone of research endeavors in the health and social sciences. Historically, however, the academic statistics community has been skeptical of analyses aimed at establishing causality. The emergence of data science presents a unique opportunity to redefine the paradigm of data analysis, seamlessly integrating causal inference from observational data. By categorizing data science contributions into three distinct task classes – descriptive analytics, predictive modeling, and counterfactual prediction (encompassing causal inference) – we establish a framework for systematically addressing the data requirements, assumptions, and analytical methodologies essential to each task. A clear taxonomy of data science tasks is crucial for clarifying the interplay between data, assumptions, and analytics, and for highlighting the pivotal role of domain expertise in data analysis – a factor often overlooked, leading to widespread misconceptions about the data science discipline.

Roxy Music will be headlining at the Isle of Wight Music Festival in June, alongside Morrissey, Supergrass, and Idlewild. The three-day event will take place from June 10-12. The original band members, Bryan Ferry, Phil Manzerana, Andy McKay, and Paul Thompson, will perform on stage on the Saturday night of the festival. Roxy Music is also working on new material, their first since the 1982 album Avalon. Formed in 1971 by Bryan Ferry, Andy McKay, and Brian Eno, with Phil Manzerana joining the following year, Roxy Music gained popularity with their hits such as "Virginia Plain" in 1972, followed by "Street Life", "Love is the Drug", and their 1981 cover of John Lennon's "Jealous Guy".

Low-symmetry 2D materials, including resorbed and reshifted monolayers, black phosphorus, group-IV monochalcogenides, and borophene, exhibit more intricate atomic arrangements than the hexagonal lattices of graphene, hexagonal boron nitride, and transition metal dichalcogenides. The reduced symmetries of these emerging materials lead to a diverse range of electronic, optical, valley, and spin responses, including ferroelasticity, ferroelectricity, magnetism, spin-wave phenomena, large nonlinear optical properties, photogalvanic effects, and superconductivity. Additionally, these materials can exhibit novel electronic topological properties, nonlinear elastic properties, and structural phase transformations due to their low symmetry. The "Beyond Graphene: Low-Symmetry and Anisotropic 2D Materials" special topic brings together recent experimental and theoretical research on these innovative materials.

What does it mean to be a parent? This is not an easy question, especially in today's diverse family structures involving adoption, stepfamilies, and surrogate motherhood. I'll demonstrate that, despite the unique appearances of their families, they understand what truly matters in family life.

Embark with me on an extraordinary journey—the remarkable odyssey of the Rosetta spacecraft. My profound passion for the past two years has been guiding this pioneering mission to rendezvous with and land a probe upon a comet. To fully appreciate this endeavor, we must first understand our solar system's origins.  

Rewind 4.5 billion years: a vast nebula of gas and dust swirled in space. At its heart, our Sun ignited, while the remaining material coalesced into planets, comets, and asteroids. Scientific theory suggests that once Earth cooled from its fiery birth, comets bombarded our young planet in staggering numbers. These celestial messengers may have been crucial—delivering not only water, but also complex organic compounds. In doing so, they could have provided the very building blocks that sparked the dawn of life itself.

We consider the connection between two constructions of the mirror partner for the calabi-yau orbifold. This orbifold is defined as a quotient by some suitable subgroup $g$ of the phase symmetries of the hypersurface $ x_m $ in the weighted projective space, cut out by a quasi-homogeneous polynomial $w_m$. The first, berglund-h\"ubsch-krawitz (bhk) construction, uses another weighted projective space and the quotient of a new hypersurface $x_{m^t}$ inside it by some dual group $g^t$. In the second, batyrev construction, the mirror partner is constructed as a hypersurface in the toric variety defined by the reflexive polytope dual to the polytope associated with the original calabi-yau orbifold. We give a simple evidence of the equivalence of these two constructions.

**The Critical Role of Ranking Algorithms in Digital Ecosystems**  

Ranking algorithms serve as fundamental mechanisms shaping user experiences across digital platforms, from search engines to recommendation systems. This paper reveals a counterintuitive phenomenon inherent to popularity-based ranking systems: when fewer items report a particular signal, they disproportionately dominate overall user engagement. Termed the *few-get-richer effect*, this dynamic arises in environments with limited item diversity—such as polarized media landscapes—where visibility is determined by popularity metrics.  

Through formal analysis, we establish that the effect manifests when users exhibit a preference for top-ranked content while holding divergent inclinations toward distinct item categories. Simulation studies further explore how variations in platform design and user behavior influence the effect’s magnitude. To validate our theoretical insights, we conduct a controlled online experiment with human participants, empirically confirming our predictions.  

These findings carry significant implications for understanding digital information ecosystems, particularly in addressing the proliferation of misinformation. By elucidating the unintended consequences of popularity-driven rankings, this research underscores the need for algorithmic accountability in fostering balanced and equitable online discourse.

She cautioned that the proposed reforms could lead to policing becoming a "political football," with those holding extreme views dictating policy on the ground. Berry's concerns highlight the need for a balanced approach, one that prioritizes the needs of the community while safeguarding the integrity of the policing service.

Electrons are conventionally regarded as possessing a single form of kinetic energy, but the possibility of multiple forms arising from their spin, charge, or interactions with other electrons warrants exploration. In one-dimensional systems, the Tomonaga-Luttinger-Liquid (TLL) model effectively captures the physics of interacting electrons at low energies, yet experimental observations have been limited to this linear regime. Our research presents a breakthrough in this area, utilizing tunnelling spectroscopy to investigate many-body modes in one-dimensional gated wires. Notably, our findings reveal the coexistence of two distinct Fermi seas at high energies, corresponding to spin and charge excitations, alongside the emergence of three additional one-dimensional 'replica' modes that intensify as the wire length decreases. Furthermore, we demonstrate the ability to modulate the effective interaction strength within the wires by adjusting the degree of one-dimensional inter-subband screening, achieving a variation of over 45%.

Nonlinear fluorescence emission has become a valuable tool for achieving high spatial resolution in optical imaging. In this study, we explored the fluorescence anomalous saturating effect associated with nitrogen vacancy defects in diamond. When exposed to high-power laser excitation, we observed a reduction in fluorescence that increased the nonlinearity of the emission. This change altered the spatial frequency distribution of the resulting fluorescence image. To capture detailed high spatial frequency information, we employed a differential excitation protocol, which involved modulating the power of the excitation laser. This approach enhanced the spatial resolution of imaging by approximately 1.6 times compared to conventional confocal microscopy. Owing to the straightforward nature of both the experimental setup and data processing, we anticipate that this technique will be highly beneficial for increasing the spatial resolution in applications such as sensing and biological labeling using defects in solids.

I believe it's rare to find someone who only has a single passion they want to pursue for their entire life. In the current workforce, about 15% of American workers are not in traditional full-time employment. Instead, they are working as part-time employees, contractors, or temporary staff. The concept of a "side hustle" aligns well with this trend, where individuals combine various roles to earn a living. The term "side hustle" originally appeared in popular African American newspapers; during the 1920s, "hustle" was used to describe a scam. However, by the 1950s, "side hustle" was commonly used to describe legitimate work. Unlike a second job, which often arises out of necessity, a side hustle is more about exploring diverse interests and opportunities.

My path to becoming an expert in polar regions—capturing their essence through photography—took root in early childhood. At just four years old, my family relocated from southern Canada to the remote northern reaches of Baffin Island, near Greenland. There, we became part of a small Inuit settlement of merely 200 people, where we stood out as one of only three non-Inuit families.  

Cut off from modern conveniences—no television, computers, radios, or even telephones—my world revolved entirely around the outdoors. The Inuit became my mentors, and the vast expanse of snow and ice transformed into my playground. It was in this untouched wilderness that my lifelong passion for the polar world was born—an obsession that has shaped my life ever since.

Enthusiasts of the Magic: The Gathering universe, be warned: Wizards of the Coast appears to have lost sight of the game's fundamental social essence in its digital iteration. At its heart, Magic is a communal experience, meant to be savored with friends and acquaintances, replete with lively banter, good-natured teasing, and the thrill of landing a game-changing blow. This is unquestionably my all-time favorite first-person shooter game. It's not designed for those who enjoy reckless, run-and-gun tactics. The recoil and ballistics are incredibly realistic. A single bullet to the chest, neck, or head from a rifle-caliber weapon usually results in death, unless it's just a graze. Bullets drop over long distances, and maintaining accuracy requires short, controlled bursts. There's no infinite supply of ammunition—you'll end up with partially filled magazines if you consistently reload halfway through. Sprinting endlessly isn't possible either; after running for a while, your aim will falter as you catch your breath. To improve steadiness, rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or use a window sill for support when aiming. Standing accuracy is less reliable.

Chinese dam firm 'defies beijing' the china three gorges project corp is refusing to obey a government order to stop construction of one of its giant dams, the chinese state press has said. The builder of the three gorges dam is continuing work on the sister xiluodu dam, said the beijing news. The xiluodu dam is one of 30 such large-scale construction projects called to a halt because of a lack of proper environmental checks. The beijing news said the company may instead choose to pay a fine. The firm has also ignored orders to stop construction at two of its other projects - the three gorges underground power plant and the three gorges project electrical power supply plant. So far, only 22 of the 30 construction projects targeted by china's state environmental protection agency (sepa) for having not carried out mandatory environmental impact assessments have complied with its shutdown order.

The notion of criminalizing flag desecration struck me as intriguing—perhaps even a potential defense against accusations of witchcraft. As an international blogger, I’ve generally hesitated to engage in certain cultural debates, especially those involving national symbols. However, having initiated this discourse (the conversation itself, not the act of burning flags), I feel compelled to share my perspective.  

Fundamentally, legislating against flag-burning is absurd. When we grant any symbol—regardless of its significance—such unquestioning veneration and legal safeguarding, we inch dangerously closer to authoritarianism. Symbols are inherently mutable; their power lies not in their intrinsic value but in the meanings we ascribe to them or impose upon them.

The long arm of anti-spam legislation has finally caught up with one of the internet's most notorious offenders. Scott Richter, the self-proclaimed "Spam King" behind OptInRealBig.com, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection following mounting legal pressure. His company, responsible for flooding inboxes with billions of unsolicited emails, claims the bankruptcy filing will allow continued operations while addressing multiple lawsuits—most significantly a multi-million dollar case brought by Microsoft.

Ranked as the world's third-largest spam operation by anti-spam organization Spamhaus, OptInRealBig first faced legal action in December 2003. Microsoft joined forces with then-New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer to prosecute Richter for massive violations of anti-spam regulations. The case revealed Richter's network had exploited 514 compromised IP addresses across 35 countries to distribute billions of illegal spam messages. This landmark litigation demonstrates how aggressive enforcement can hold even the most prolific spammers accountable for their actions.

A Georgia woman has filed a class-action lawsuit against Hewlett-Packard (HP), alleging that the company deliberately programs its printer ink cartridges to deactivate prematurely. According to the complaint, an embedded microchip falsely signals that the cartridge is empty, forcing users to replace it even when ink remains. The lawsuit aims to include all U.S. consumers who purchased HP inkjet printers since February 2001.  

HP, the global leader in printer manufacturing, has declined to address the allegations. While the company states that its cartridge chips are designed to monitor ink levels and prompt timely replacements, the lawsuit contends that these chips are also programmed to disable cartridges on a preset expiration date, irrespective of actual ink supply.

The emaciated orphan pleading for another meager portion of gruel. The lovelorn maiden fading into oblivion while clinging to her threadbare bridal gown. The callous miser tormented by spectral visions of bygone holidays. Even after a hundred years, these vivid characters from Charles Dickens' literary universe remain indelibly etched in our collective consciousness. His extraordinary oeuvre was so distinctive that it spawned its own descriptive term in the English lexicon. Yet what quintessential elements render Dickens' prose so remarkably enduring?

Dickens' narratives pulsate with suspense, masterfully crafted through atmospheric settings brimming with foreboding, unexpected narrative turns, and tantalizing enigmas. These signature techniques created an insatiable hunger among his readership, compelling them to eagerly await each new installment. Originally, his tales unfolded serially - published chapter by chapter in inexpensive periodicals before being compiled into bound volumes, a format that perfectly suited his suspenseful storytelling genius.

**The Digital Revolution: How High-Speed Internet is Transforming Online Engagement**  

Research indicates that broadband connectivity is revolutionizing how individuals interact online, fostering unprecedented levels of digital self-expression. A study by the UK-based think tank Demos reveals that 25% of British broadband users actively publish content and maintain personal websites, underscoring the profound influence of instant, high-speed access.  

The report highlights how always-on, rapid connections are reshaping internet behavior, with over five million UK households now subscribed to broadband—a figure rising steadily. Findings show that more than half of broadband users access the web before breakfast, while one in five admit to late-night browsing sessions. Most notably, broadband is empowering users to transition from passive consumers to active participants, fundamentally altering the digital landscape.

The history of the Milky Way's assembly is gradually being uncovered, thanks to data from the Gaia mission. It appears that the globular cluster system within our galaxy has followed an equally complex formation path. To better understand this formation, we investigate the connection between these globular clusters and the known merger events that the Milky Way has undergone. By integrating Gaia's kinematic data for nearly all galactic clusters with the most comprehensive and systematically corrected sample of cluster ages, we identify clusters that likely share a common origin through an analysis of their dynamical properties, specifically within the context of integrals of motion. Our findings suggest that approximately 40% of these clusters likely originated within the galaxy itself.

In the framework of general relativity, the Kerr spacetime stands as the singular stationary solution for an isolated black hole, characterized by a distinctive multipolar structure that is solely determined by its mass and spin. This work introduces a novel methodology for extracting multipole moments from arbitrary stationary spacetimes, which is subsequently applied to a vast array of horizonless microstate geometries. Notably, these geometries can disrupt the axial and equatorial symmetries inherent to the Kerr metric, thereby exhibiting a far more intricate multipolar structure. This, in turn, offers a unique opportunity to phenomenologically constrain fuzzball models. Our analysis reveals compelling numerical evidence suggesting that the multipole moments of these geometries are generally more pronounced than those of a Kerr black hole with equivalent mass and spin. Presently, measurements of the quadrupole moment of black-hole candidates impose only modest constraints on fuzzball models. However, forthcoming gravitational-wave detections of extreme mass-ratio inspirals via the LISA space mission are poised to significantly enhance these bounds, yielding improvements of several orders of magnitude.

Acclaimed author Andrea Levy, already an Orange Prize winner, has secured the Whitbread Novel of the Year Award for her celebrated work *Small Island*. This victory positions her as the frontrunner for the prestigious Whitbread Book of the Year title, surpassing even Booker Prize winner Alan Hollinghurst’s *The Line of Beauty*.

Bookmakers William Hill have already tipped Levy’s London-set novel as the favorite at 6/4 odds.

Entanglement is a key resource for quantum information processing. A widely used tool for detecting entanglement is entanglement witness, where the measurement of the witness operator is guaranteed to be positive for all separable states and can be negative for certain entangled states. In reality, due to the exponentially increasing the hilbert-space dimension with respective to the system size, it is very challenging to construct an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled states. For $n$-partite greenberger-horne-zeilinger (ghz)-like states, the most robust witness scheme requires $n+1$ local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/2$ white noise. As a comparison, the most efficient witness for ghz-like states only needs two local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/3$ white noise.

**Subject:** Urgent Concern Regarding PX Day-Ahead Auction Disruptions Due to ISO Rule Changes  

**Date:** Monday, December 11, 2000  
**To:** All PX Market Participants  
**From:** John Yurkanin, Chief Operating Officer  

Dear Market Participants,  

The California ISO's emergency tariff filing last Friday has significantly disrupted the PX Day-Ahead Auction market. The simultaneous implementation of soft and hard caps has created a fundamental disconnect, exposing all participants to unacceptable risks. Furthermore, these changes are unlikely to achieve the ISO's intended reduction in real-time energy sales and procurement.  

As a result, supply resources will continue prioritizing the ISO's higher-priced real-time opportunities. To address this issue, the PX is formally requesting that Terry Winter of the ISO authorize immediate adjustment bids for all submitted schedules at market-relevant prices.  

We appreciate your attention to this critical matter and will keep you informed of further developments.  

Best regards,  
John Yurkanin  
Chief Operating Officer

Density-based clustering relies on the idea of linking groups to some specific features of the probability distribution underlying the data. The reference to a true, yet unknown, population structure allows to frame the clustering problem in a standard inferential setting, where the concept of ideal population clustering is defined as the partition induced by the true density function. The nonparametric formulation of this approach, known as modal clustering, draws a correspondence between the groups and the domains of attraction of the density modes. Operationally, a nonparametric density estimate is required and a proper selection of the amount of smoothing, governing the shape of the density and hence possibly the modal structure, is crucial to identify the final partition. In this work, we address the issue of density estimation for modal clustering from an asymptotic perspective.

In this paper, we explore multi-agent discrete-event systems with partial observation. We divide the agents into groups, each with similar state transition structures, and label them using a common template. A scalable supervisor, whose state size and computational cost are independent of the number of agents, is designed for partial observation. The scalable supervisor does not need to be recomputed, regardless of how many agents are added or removed from the system. We extend our previous results to partial observation by proposing sufficient conditions for safety and maximal permissiveness of the scalable least restrictive supervisor on the template level. An example is provided to illustrate the proposed scalable supervisory synthesis.

Deep hashing methods have demonstrated remarkable retrieval accuracy and efficiency in large-scale image retrieval. Optimizing discrete hash bits is the primary focus in deep hashing methods. A common approach in these methods is to adopt an activation function, such as the sigmoid or tanh function, and minimize a quantization loss to approximate discrete values. However, this paradigm may result in more and more hash bits being stuck in the saturated area of the activation functions and never escaping. We refer to this problem as the "dead bits problem." Moreover, the existing quantization loss will exacerbate this issue. In this paper, we propose a simple yet effective gradient amplifier that acts before activation functions to alleviate this problem, thereby alleviating the "dead bits problem.

Hands down my favorite first person shooter of all time. Run and gun rambos need not apply. Recoil and ballistics are realistic. Single hits to the chest, neck, or head from rifle caliber weapons almost always result in death unless it just grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are required if you wish to remain on target. There is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload half-way through your magazine all the time then you will have a pouch full of half full mags. You can't run forever. If you sprint for a while your aim will suffer as you gasp heavily until you have caught your breath. Rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a window sill to steady your aim. Accuracy suffers when standing.

The UK's opposition Conservative Party has announced a proposal for mandatory health checks for immigrants if they secure victory in the upcoming general election. This initiative targets individuals looking to live and work in Britain from non-EU countries. Those intending to stay for six months or longer—and who are originating from regions with high tuberculosis rates—would be required to undergo a chest X-ray and additional tests as needed. Furthermore, all non-EU residents planning to remain for a year or more would be subject to a comprehensive medical examination for tuberculosis, hepatitis B, and HIV. A positive tuberculosis diagnosis would lead to an automatic rejection of visa applications, while other medical conditions would be evaluated on an individual basis.

Sun offers processing by the hour sun microsystems has launched a pay-as-you-go service which will allow customers requiring huge computing power to rent it by the hour. Sun grid costs users $1 (53p) for an hour's worth of processing and storage power on systems maintained by sun. So-called grid computing is the latest buzz phrase in a company which believes that computing capacity is as important a commodity as hardware and software. Sun likened grid computing to the development of electricity. The system could mature in the same way utilities such as electricity and water have developed, said sun's chief operating officer jonathan schwartz. "why build your own grid when you can use ours for a buck an hour?" he asked in a webcast launching sun's quarterly network computing event in california.

Creating a comprehensive water weather service is essential, yet achieving this vision requires collective advocacy for accountability—without which securing necessary funding remains unlikely.  

Reflecting on my journey, I recall my first TED talk eight years ago, fresh from graduate school. In those brief moments on stage, I unknowingly defined the questions that would shape the next decade of my work. Like many in their twenties, I was driven by idealism, convinced that technology alone could solve the world’s most pressing water challenges. I had much to learn.  

It was tempting to believe that the greatest obstacles in water quality stemmed from difficulties in detection—and that the solution lay in developing simpler, faster, and cheaper sensors. But I soon realized how mistaken I was.

The dichotomy between two twin domes, one crafted from thousands of steel components and the other from a single silk thread, highlights the fundamental contrast between synthetic and organic design approaches. One is imposed upon the environment, while the other creates it. Michelangelo once gazed upon raw marble, he saw a figure struggling to be set free. The chisel was Michelangelo's only tool. However, living things do not conform to the chisel. They grow. At the most fundamental level, our cells carry all the information necessary for every other cell to function and replicate. Similarly, tools also have consequences. Since the industrial revolution, the world of design has been dominated by the rigors of manufacturing and mass production, leading to a profound impact on the way we approach design. This has resulted in a world where the boundaries between creator and creation are increasingly blurred, and the value of human craftsmanship is often overlooked.

Density-based clustering is built on the concept of connecting groups to specific characteristics of the probability distribution that underlies the data. By referring to the true, albeit unknown, population structure, the clustering problem can be cast into a standard inferential framework. Within this framework, the notion of an ideal population clustering is defined as the partition created by the true density function. The nonparametric approach to this idea, known as modal clustering, links the groups to the domains of attraction of the density modes. In practice, this requires a nonparametric density estimate, and appropriately selecting the degree of smoothing is crucial, as it determines the shape of the density—and thus the possible modal structure—to identify the final partition. This study examines the challenges of density estimation for modal clustering from an asymptotic perspective.

Pros: The graphics are impressive.

Cons: The game has several issues. Bugs are prevalent, particularly in the environment, which can cause your character to get stuck or result in your shots hitting invisible barriers despite having a clear line of sight to the enemy. The gameplay feels overly guided, offering too much handholding. The artificial intelligence appears poorly designed, and enemies always seem to know your location once you're spotted. Their accuracy is unrealistically perfect, reminiscent of the frustrating precision seen in the first Ghost Warrior game. The checkpoint and save system is poorly implemented, and the story is dull and uninspiring. I wouldn’t recommend this game unless it’s heavily discounted—at least 90% off. Even then, playing it feels like a tedious chore, endured only to move on to another game in my collection. Given the time I found myself stuck in the first Ghost Warrior (which was equally monotonous, providing relief only upon its completion), I doubt I'll play this one through to the end.

I can't think of anyone who just has one interest in life, and that's all they want to do for the rest of their life. [the way we work]around 15 percent of american workers don't have traditional full-time jobs. They're half-time, part-time, contract workers or temps. The term "side hustle" just seems to fit with this ethos where people are putting together a few different things to make a living.the word "side hustle" has its roots in popular african american newspapers. In the 1920s, these papers used the word "hustle" to refer to some kind of scam. By the 1950s, they were using "side hustle" to refer to legitimate work, too. A side hustle is a little different than a second job. A second job is about necessity.

Manchester United narrowed Chelsea's Premier League advantage to nine points with a hard-fought 2-0 victory over Manchester City at the Etihad Stadium. The breakthrough came when Wayne Rooney cleverly redirected Gary Neville's cross at the near post, with the ball taking a decisive deflection off Richard Dunne to beat David James. The City defender's misfortune continued seven minutes later as he inadvertently volleyed the ball over his own goalkeeper's head for United's second. 

Despite dominating possession, City squandered their best opportunity when Steve McManaman fired wide from point-blank range in the first half. United initially struggled to impose themselves, displaying more effort than finesse in their build-up play during the opening period. Paul Scholes' wayward header represented their only genuine chance before the interval in what appeared to be an unconvincing performance from Sir Alex Ferguson's side. However, their second-half resurgence ultimately secured three crucial points in their pursuit of league leaders Chelsea.

We present a new mixed variable symplectic (mvs) integrator for planetary systems, that fully resolve close encounters. The method is based on a time regularisation that allows keeping the stability properties of the symplectic integrators, while also reducing the effective step size whenever two planets encounter. We use a high order mvs scheme such that it is possible to integrate with large time steps far away from close encounters. We show that this algorithm is able to resolve almost exact collisions (i.e with a mutual separation of a fraction of the physical radius) while using the same time-step as in weakly perturbed problem such as the solar system. We demonstrate the long term behaviour on systems of six super-earths experiencing strong scattering for 50 kyr. We compare our algorithm to hybrid methods such as mercury and show that for an equivalent cost we obtain much better energy conservation.
