Other notable new entries in the top ten include Girls Aloud's Wake Me Up, which debuted at number four, narrowly missing out on the top three. The latest Elvis Presley re-release, (Marie's the Name) His Latest Flame, has also made a significant impact, entering the chart at number three. 

Additionally, hip-hop artist Akon's Locked Up has secured a respectable number five position, while the highly-acclaimed Leeds band Kaiser Chiefs have achieved their second chart hit with Oh My God, which debuted at number six. The Futureheads and Usher also feature in the top ten, further solidifying their presence in the UK music scene.

A comprehensive investigation into the orientational dynamics of a comb-shaped nematic polymer, featuring both mesogenic and functional side groups, was conducted in its isotropic phase using a combination of Kerr effect and dielectric spectroscopy techniques. Notably, this study revealed a significant departure from the behavior of low-molecular-weight mesogens, as the relaxation of electric birefringence in the polymer melt above the nematic-isotropic phase transition temperature could be accurately described by a multi-exponential decay process, with two dominant relaxation mechanisms playing a crucial role. These primary processes exhibit a temperature-dependent crossover, occurring within a range of approximately 50 degrees. Furthermore, dielectric spectroscopy enabled the identification of two distinct orientational relaxation processes: one attributed to the rotational motion of the side mesogenic groups, and the other associated with the movement of main chain segments.

Firstly, while it is true that the economic stimulus package has been implemented, its methodology is fundamentally flawed. As Robert Rubin astutely pointed out in a recent interview with NPR's Marketplace, the administration could have achieved the same level of economic stimulation without incurring substantial long-term debt by opting for temporary, targeted tax cuts that would have disproportionately benefited low- and middle-income individuals. The wisdom and expertise of Robert Rubin are a breath of fresh air, and his presence is a stark reminder of the dearth of similarly esteemed individuals within the current administration's domestic policy team. Secondly, it is imperative to recognize that granting the Republicans unchecked control during wartime is no longer a viable option, given their ingenious yet troubling ability to perpetuate a state of endless, global conflict under the guise of the 'war on terrorism.'

Spontaneous conversations in real-world settings such as those found in child-centered recordings have been shown to be amongst the most challenging audio files to process. Nevertheless, building speech processing models handling such a wide variety of conditions would be particularly useful for language acquisition studies in which researchers are interested in the quantity and quality of the speech that children hear and produce, as well as for early diagnosis and measuring effects of remediation. In this paper, we present our approach to designing an open-source neural network to classify audio segments into vocalizations produced by the child wearing the recording device, vocalizations produced by other children, adult male speech, and adult female speech. To this end, we gathered diverse child-centered corpora which sums up to a total of 260 hours of recordings and covers 10 languages.

From the moment of its release, I encountered no performance issues, and with subsequent updates, the game now runs exceptionally smoothly. While the format—particularly the way enemies respawn in the open-world city after story progression—can feel repetitive, this remains an outstanding and thoroughly enjoyable experience. The "dual play" mechanics, allowing you to team up as Batman alongside another character, were a standout feature. I also appreciated the seamless transition between controller use for general gameplay and mouse-and-keyboard controls for tank battles. Navigating Gotham in the Batmobile, especially during high-speed pursuits like chasing APC commanders, becomes remarkably satisfying once you master the art of drifting around corners—a skill that transforms the entire driving experience into pure exhilaration.

Extreme Mass Ratio Inspirals (EMRIs) can be categorized into two distinct types based on their formation processes: dry EMRIs and wet EMRIs. Dry EMRIs, traditionally regarded as the primary EMRI sources detectable by the Laser Interferometer Space Antenna (LISA), typically arise from multi-body interactions within nuclear star clusters and gravitational capture events.  

In this study, we present a novel EMRI formation pathway: wet EMRIs facilitated by the accretion disks surrounding actively feeding supermassive black holes (SMBHs) in galactic centers. Within this mechanism, stellar-mass black holes on initially inclined orbits are gravitationally captured by the accretion disk. The disk then induces orbital migration, efficiently guiding these compact objects toward the central SMBH. This process dramatically enhances EMRI formation rates in such galactic environments, potentially increasing them by several orders of magnitude.

Sustainability represents the what, the where and the how of what is caught. The who and the why are what's important to me. I want to know the people behind my dinner choices. I want to know how i impact them. I want to know how they impact me. I want to know why they fish. I want to know how they rely on the water's bounty for their living. Understanding all of this enables us to shift our perception of seafood away from a commodity to an opportunity to restore our ecosystem. It allows for us to celebrate the seafood that we're also so fortunate to eat.so what do we call this? i think we call it restorative seafood. Where sustainability is the capacity to endure and maintain, restorative is the ability to replenish and progress.

Hands down, my favorite first-person shooter of all time. The run-and-gun playstyle, which is often associated with this genre, need not apply. The recoil and ballistics are remarkably realistic. A single hit to the chest, neck, or head from a rifle-caliber weapon almost always results in death, unless it merely grazes you. Rounds drop over distance and short, controlled bursts are essential if you wish to remain on target. It's crucial to be mindful of your ammunition supply, as there is no magical ammunition pool. If you reload halfway through your magazine, you'll be left with a pouch full of half-full mags. It's crucial to pace yourself, as sprinting for an extended period will inevitably lead to a decline in your aim. To maintain accuracy, it's essential to rest your weapon on your elbows while prone or on a window sill, allowing your aim to stabilize. Your accuracy will suffer if you stand upright, as the adrenaline rush will impede your ability to focus and control your breathing.

Fallout 3 story was better. Settlements get in the way of the story to the point you'll hate baby sitting them instead of playing the story. Some keys are hard coded and cannot be rebound. Bugs galore. My pipboy disappeared permanently at one point and had to research console commands to get it back. You don't have to work for power armor it's basically given to you from the beginning of the game and power cores are everywhere in trader shops. Value of power armor diminished greatly from previous fallout games. Story is medicore, everything about this game is mediocre except it doesn't crash every 10 mins like fallout 3. Very stable compared to fallout 3. Is it better than fallout 3 no. Is it better than new vegas? no. This title was so overhyped for how bad it actually is.

Does it even need to be said why Portal is an extraordinary achievement? Portal is a puzzle game that presents a concept, then promptly disappears, leaving you with an intellectual challenge that is both entertaining and mentally stimulating. Although this game may not be narrative-driven, it has given rise to one of the most beloved games of 2011, and I still find myself returning to it when I'm feeling under-stimulated, as I'm drawn to explore the origins of a phenomenon and the enduring legend surrounding it. Gameplay  Portal's gameplay is refreshingly straightforward, at its core. You utilize portals to your advantage, in combination with buttons that activate specific puzzle elements. There are two buttons in the game - one that you press, and one that requires a cube to be applied.

Cardinal Cormac Murphy-O'Connor has voiced strong criticism regarding the vast sums of money spent on the Iraq war and conflicts in the Middle East, arguing that these funds would have been better used to alleviate poverty. As the leader of the Catholic Church in England and Wales, he shared his thoughts on BBC Radio 4 and planned to emphasize his perspective during his Christmas Midnight Mass. In his Christmas address, he condemned the Iraq war as a "terrible" misuse of resources. Both he and the Archbishop of Canterbury have publicly opposed the war. On the BBC Radio 4's Thought for the Day segment, the cardinal highlighted the misallocation of "billions" towards warfare instead of efforts to lift people out of severe poverty, malnourishment, and disease. He urged for 2005 to become a pivotal year for campaigning to "make poverty history.

I've had the privilege of playing this game for only a few months, and in that relatively short period, two DLCs have already been released, accompanied by patches that have transformed an already impressive game into an outstanding one. The developers' commitment to the community is evident in the numerous discussion forums and developer diaries, where their dedication to the game's growth is palpable. While the game's mechanics may present a steep learning curve, the sheer breadth of strategic options available to me allows me to experiment with various approaches and capitalize on specific situations. In my opinion, the game is almost infinitely replayable, and I find myself becoming increasingly engrossed in each session, regardless of the play-through outcome.

Causal inference from observational data is a primary goal of numerous health and social science data analyses. Traditionally, academic statisticians have often viewed analyses with causal objectives skeptically. However, the emergence of "data science" offers a pivotal chance to redefine data analysis, seamlessly integrating causal inference from observational data. As others have done, we categorize the contributions of data science into three main tasks: description, prediction, and counterfactual prediction (encompassing causal inference). Clearly defining these tasks is essential for discussing the requisite data, assumptions, and analytics needed for each. We assert that inadequately highlighting the role of domain-specific expert knowledge in data analysis contributes significantly to widespread misconceptions about data science.

I initially purchased this game during its early development phase, back when "spacebux" was the sole currency and there wasn't a "tech tree." At that time, the game truly shone. The weapons were all equally overpowered, which eliminated any issues of imbalance—any player could defeat any other at any moment. Additionally, the game used to offer unlimited gun slots and five loadouts. Considering the game's premise of featuring "bajillions of guns," you would expect it to provide more than just six weapon slots and three loadouts before requiring additional payment to unlock more. I once had an arsenal spanning three pages and was brimming with new ideas for expansion, as that was where I personally found the excitement and enjoyment.

For the first time, digital music downloads are being included in the main US singles chart. Billboard's Hot 100 chart now incorporates data from digital music sales, which were previously tracked only on a separate download chart. Currently, Green Day's "Boulevard of Broken Dreams" is positioned at number two on Billboard's Pop Chart and leads the Digital Chart. The incorporation of download sales into the UK singles chart is planned for later this year. In the US, digital sales already contribute to the creation of Billboard's Hot Digital Sales Chart. Now, these digital sales will be combined with physical single sales and airplay data to form the updated Hot 100 chart. Another new chart, the Pop 100, also merges airplay, digital, and physical sales but limits its airplay data to US radio stations that feature chart music.

I apologize, but I cannot reveal my face, as doing so would put me at risk from those who seek to harm me. My journey began 14 years ago when I was a newly graduated young reporter. One day, I stumbled upon what seemed like a straightforward story: police officers accepting bribes from street vendors. Although this issue was widely known, no efforts had effectively addressed it. As an ambitious young journalist, I aimed to present the story in a unique and impactful manner. To achieve this, I decided to go undercover as a vendor, allowing me to gather irrefutable evidence of the wrongdoing. The resulting impact was remarkable and powerful. This approach is often referred to as immersion journalism or undercover journalism.

We have developed single-shot shaped pulses designed for ultra-high fidelity (uh-fidelity) population transfer within a three-level quantum system configured in a lambda arrangement. To achieve high fidelity, we employ the Lewis-Riesenfeld (L-R) method to derive a family of solutions that facilitate exact transfer, ensuring that these solutions adhere to a single dynamical mode of the L-R invariant. From this family, we identify a tracking solution characterized by a single parameter, which effectively controls the fidelity of the transfer, the population within the excited state, and the robustness of the system. We define the robustness of an uh-fidelity transfer as the minimum percentile deviation in pulse areas that results in infidelity exceeding $10^{-4}$. Our shaped pulses demonstrate superior robustness compared to Gaussian and adiabatically-optimized pulses when considering moderate pulse areas.

Renowned scholar Arun Shourie draws attention to the prescient warning issued by the current Governor of Uttar Pradesh, dating back to 1996, regarding the looming threat of a 'Third Islamic State' emerging in the subcontinent. T.V. Rajeswar, the former Director of the Intelligence Bureau and current Governor of Uttar Pradesh, had previously sounded the alarm in a series of articles published in the Hindustan Times. Shourie poses a pertinent question  does the revelation that Rajeswar, rather than himself or Mr. Advani, was the author of these warnings lend credibility to the predictions, or does it instead lead one to conclude that Rajeswar is a closet communalist, thereby disqualifying him from continuing as Governor of Uttar Pradesh?

Sir Alex Ferguson lauded Manchester United's resilience following their hard-fought 1-0 victory against Aston Villa, describing it as their most challenging away fixture this season. "It was an exceptional match with relentless action and superb technical play from both sides," remarked the legendary Old Trafford manager. "The team demonstrated tremendous fortitude and determination – we simply refused to accept defeat."

Ferguson highlighted the significance of claiming three points at Villa Park, noting, "We've successfully navigated this difficult away test, while our title rivals Arsenal and Chelsea still face this challenge – potentially against a strengthened Villa squad." The Scottish tactician reserved special praise for veteran substitutes Ryan Giggs and Roy Keane, who replaced the injured John O'Shea. "Roy's introduction brought much-needed stability and leadership to our midfield – qualities uniquely his own," Ferguson observed, acknowledging Keane's invaluable contribution to the victory.

Podcasting is the creation and distribution of audio content over the internet, typically through a series of episodes that can be downloaded or streamed by listeners. The term "podcasting" was coined to describe the practice of creating and distributing audio content using an Apple iPod, but it has since become a broader term to refer to any type of digital audio content.
Podcasters create and produce their shows in a variety of formats, including interviews, news, music, and storytelling, and they can use a variety of platforms and tools to distribute their content.

Adam Curry is a well-known proponent of podcasting and has been credited with coining the term "podcasting." He was a host and VJ (video jockey) on MTV, and he began creating and distributing his own podcasts in the early 2000s. Curry's podcasts covered a wide range of topics, including music, politics, and culture, and they quickly gained popularity due to their unique blend of humor and insight.

Podcasting has become a popular medium for creators to share their ideas and perspectives with a global audience. It allows them to produce high-quality audio content without the need for expensive studios or equipment, and it provides a level of flexibility and creative control that is not possible with traditional radio or television.

February 7th, 2014 - I'm afraid I cannot wholeheartedly endorse/enjoy this game due to two significant issues I've encountered during my brief playtime. Firstly, the lack of Australian servers is a major hindrance, as even a single red bar can cause unbearable lag and make the game unplayable. I experienced this issue even during offline training mode, which stuttered and lagged as well (how does that happen?). Coupled with the poor ping, it's a real disappointment. Secondly, the game exhibits extreme artifacting, affecting menus, backgrounds, training, and in-game elements. I've never experienced this issue with this card before, and I thought it might be due to my overclocking. However, even resetting my GPU to stock settings didn't make a difference.

We introduce a novel skyrme-like conserved current in the effective theory of pions and vector mesons based on the idea of hidden local symmetry. The associated charge is equivalent to the skyrmion charge for any smooth configuration. In addition, there exist singular configurations that can be identified as n_f=1 baryons charged under the new symmetry. Under this identification, the vector mesons play the role of the chern-simons vector fields living on the quantum hall droplet that forms the n_f=1 baryon. We propose that this current is the correct effective expression for the baryon current at low energies. This proposal gives a unified picture for the two types of baryons and allows them to continuously transform one to the other in a natural way. In addition, chern-simons dualities on the droplet can be interpreted as a result of seiberg-like duality between gluons and vector mesons.

Hello. This is my first trip outside of Gaza and it's my first time leaving the city. I'm so happy to be here. (applause) My ambition has always been to be a pilot and fly a plane, to feel free to soar through the sky, to touch the sky. But that didn't happen. Unfortunately, I live in Gaza, and there is no airport. All borders are closed on every side. We live in one of the biggest prisons in the world. The only thing I can do is just look up to the sky. On some days, we are lucky if we have electricity for four or five hours. When it's cold, we make a fire on the front or on the roof of our homes.

Brizzel to run aaa's in sheffield ballymena sprinter paul brizzel will be among eight of ireland's european indoor hopefuls competing in this weekend's aaa's championships. Us-based alistair cragg and mark carroll are the only irish athletes selected so far for the europeans who will not run in sheffield. Brizzel will defend his 200m title in the british trials. In-form james mcilroy will hope to confirm his place in the british team for madrid by winning the 800m title. Mcilroy has been in tremendous form on the european circuit in recent weeks. He is one of the fastest 800m runners in the world this winter and already seems assured of a place in madrid. Corkman mark carroll confirmed in midweek that he would join cragg in the european championships. Carroll is ranked number three in the world 3000m ranking at the moment with cragg occupying top spot.

Today... I miss my sister terribly - it just seems strange not to have her hanging around, irritating me (just joking...). Even though it was only for a week, she really made her presence felt, and i mean that in a good way. Just doing the scrapbook for our trtip to tasmania brings fond memories of a time past recently - last week in fact. Maybe it's just the thought that i won't see her for quite a while - with her going to bristol in sept/oct and all. She represented, on a deeper level, a link to my family back home. Oh how i miss them all. Tomorrow is the start of a new term. How did the holidays fly by so quickly?

Scotland's Justice Minister, Jamie Jamieson, has issued a stern warning to bigoted soccer fans, stating that she intends to take action against them 'where it hurts most' by banning them from matches. This is part of a series of measures being considered to combat sectarianism in the Scottish Executive's campaign. Jamieson praised Celtic and Rangers for their efforts in tackling the issue, stating that stopping sectarian abuse linked to old firm matches is a key objective. The minister was speaking ahead of the third round Scottish Cup clash between the Glasgow clubs at Parkhead on Sunday. The sectarianism long associated with sections of the support from both clubs has become a significant target for the executive. Last week, Jamieson and First Minister Jack McConnel met with supporters' representatives from both clubs to discuss the issue.

On the Saturday, we took Connor to Fireman's Park, anticipating that the Easter egg hunt was scheduled to begin at 11 am. However, it turned out that it was actually on the previous day, Friday. I'm grateful I brought a bag of eggs with me, just in case. As a result, Connor had a fantastic time searching for eggs throughout the park. The following morning, when he woke up, he was ecstatic to discover that the Easter bunny had indeed arrived. There's nothing more heartwarming than witnessing the excitement on a child's face. Following the egg hunt, we attended church, acknowledging that while the Easter bunny may be a fun and whimsical tradition, we prioritize instilling the true meaning of the holiday in our children. This was Megan's first Easter, and she looked adorable in her jean dress and stockings, beaming with joy.

Israeli club look to africa four african players, including zimbabwe goalkeeper energy murambadoro, are all ready to play for israeli club hapoel bnei sakhnin in the uefa cup. Bnei sakhnin are the first arab side ever to play in european competition and will play english premiership side newcastle united in the first round. Warriors' goalkeeper murambadoro, who made a name for himself at the african nations cup finals in tunisia, helped bnei sakhnin overcome albania's partizani tirana 6-1 in the previous round. Murambadoro moved to israel recently after a brief stint with south african club hellenic. The club won the israeli cup final last season and are based in sakhnin, which is near haifa. The club have a strong ethic and are high profile promoters of peace and co-operation within israel.

We introduce natural decompositions for the values of global Mackey functors at orthogonal, unitary, and symplectic groups. Specifically, the restriction homomorphisms linking the orthogonal, unitary, and symplectic groups across successive dimensions are inherently split epimorphisms. This splitting is particularly relevant in the context of equivariant stable homotopy theory. In this framework, the equivariant stable homotopy groups of any global spectrum organize into a global Mackey functor, making the splittings instrumental in breaking down certain long exact homotopy group sequences into shorter exact sequences. In the case of the real and complex global Thom spectra, denoted as $\mathbf{mo}$ and $\mathbf{mu}$, these splittings affirm the consistency of various Euler classes associated with the tautological representations of $o(n)$ and $u(n)$.

Imagine if you could plug your brain into a machine that would guarantee a life of unparalleled pleasure, free from the burdens of pain and suffering. The question posed by philosopher Robert Nozick is a thought-provoking one  would you choose to sign up for such an existence, where you would experience a world of simulated reality, indistinguishable from the real thing, but at the cost of leaving behind your mortal existence? The experience would be a unique one, as you would be disconnected from the natural ebbs and flows of life, replaced by an endless series of euphoric experiences. It's a tantalizing prospect, to say the least, but would you take the plunge?

An absolute great stragtegy game! having played every single other wargame (european escalation, and alb), i have to say this one fits well. The number of units are great. Not sure exactly how many units there are, but there's a crap ton!!! graphics are just beautiful, sounds are great, and the gameplay overall is epic. The ingame chat box at the main menu is very entertaining as well, no i am not kidding. However, if anyone thinking this game is 'easy', or if you are new to the strategy genre, it could take you quite some time to get it nailed down. Do not get upset, you will lose a lot of units. Just make sure you plan ahead, always have anti-air, and anti-tank.

Do you remember "Age of Mythology?" It's been quite a while since we've seen an RTS game of similar caliber. However, Divinity: Dragon Commander comes remarkably close to being one of my personal favorites in the RTS genre. It combines this with an expansive, Risk-like grand strategy mode that perfectly complements the experience. I strive to remain objective, but it's hard not to be impressed by a game that lets you transform into a dragon, assisting your troops through support, attacks, and even directing unit production, movement, and spawning simultaneously. While I appreciate the complexity of modern RTS games like Civilization V, these titles often try to pack numerous intricate mechanics, graphs, and resources into a single, convoluted gameplay mode.

This game was a delightful discovery. The reviews of certain products often feature glowing praise, but in this instance, the reviews accurately capture the enjoyable essence of this charming game. It's an intriguing blend of a classic 2D platformer and a MOBA, executed with panache. I found myself drawn to characters that I wouldn't typically appreciate in other MOBA games. Initially, I was skeptical about the potential for a melee tank, but I was pleasantly surprised by the outcome and ended up exploring more characters as they became available through continued gameplay. The art style and user interface are delightfully charming. As a result, I now use one of the characters as my avatar and a wallpaper from creating a card set for the game, showcasing my fondness for this delightful experience.

I feel uneasy about investigative reporting; it doesn't sit right with me. To clarify, the particular article I'm working on and its implications make me uncomfortable. I suspect this is because I don't fully believe in it, which prevents me from truly grasping its essence—its underlying intensity and the fervent drive to challenge the status quo. I grew up in a country where questioning authority was unheard of. Doing so could get you shot or imprisoned under strict security laws. I'm not accustomed to this freedom of speech or to openly questioning things. Admittedly, I can be a difficult person to get along with.

Nonlinear fluorescence emission has emerged as a powerful tool for achieving high-resolution optical imaging. In this study, we investigate the anomalous fluorescence saturation phenomenon in nitrogen-vacancy centers within diamond crystals. Our experiments revealed a pronounced fluorescence quenching effect under intense laser excitation, which significantly enhanced the nonlinear emission characteristics and altered the spatial frequency profile of the fluorescence images.  

To leverage this effect, we developed a differential excitation technique that effectively captures high spatial frequency components. Through precise modulation of the excitation laser power, we achieved a remarkable 1.6-fold improvement in spatial resolution compared to conventional confocal microscopy. Given the straightforward experimental implementation and minimal computational requirements, we anticipate this approach will find broad applications in enhancing spatial resolution for quantum sensing and biological labeling using solid-state defect centers.

Establishing a comprehensive weather service for water is imperative, but it will only become a reality when we collectively demand accountability and drive the necessary investment. My journey to address this issue began eight years ago, when I first took the stage at TED, fresh out of graduate school. At the time, I was unaware that my brief presentation would set the tone for the questions I would be grappling with for the next decade. Like many young idealists, I was convinced that I could single-handedly solve the world's water problems through technological innovation. However, I soon discovered that the reality was far more complex. I had initially been misled into believing that the persistence of water quality issues was primarily due to the difficulty in identifying them. I assumed that the solution lay in developing simpler, faster, and more affordable sensors. This assumption proved to be misguided, and I have since come to realize that the challenges we face are far more nuanced.

Weight pruning is universally recognized as a simple yet powerful technique for reducing redundancy in deep neural networks (DNNs), enabling accelerated performance across diverse hardware platforms. However, conventional pruning methods typically compromise between model precision and structural regularity, often resulting in diminished inference accuracy and suboptimal on-device acceleration.  

To address these limitations, we present a novel sparsity paradigm—pattern-based sparsity—which integrates both pattern and connectivity sparsity. This approach not only preserves high accuracy but also ensures hardware efficiency. Through meticulously crafted sparsity patterns, our method achieves unprecedented improvements in both model precision and feature extraction across varied DNN architectures and datasets.  

Furthermore, our pattern-aware pruning framework seamlessly combines pattern library extraction, optimal pattern selection, simultaneous pattern and connectivity pruning, and weight training. By leveraging this innovative pattern-based sparsity, our solution inherently aligns with compiler optimizations, enabling highly efficient DNN execution on mobile devices.

In this study, we investigate the topography of the negative log-likelihood function of Gaussian mixture models with a general number of components. Due to the non-convex nature of the problem, multiple local minima may exist, even when the mixture is well-separated. We demonstrate that all local minima share a common structural property, which provides partial identification of the component centers of the true mixture. Specifically, each local minimum involves a non-overlapping combination of fitting multiple Gaussians to a single true component and fitting a single Gaussian to multiple true components. Our findings are applicable to the scenario where the true mixture components satisfy a certain separation condition and remain valid even when the number of components is over- or under-specified. In the case of Gaussian mixtures with three components, we achieve sharper results in terms of the scaling between the components, as well as the separation between the components.

We examine the link between two methods for constructing the mirror partner of a Calabi-Yau orbifold. This orbifold is defined as a quotient by a suitable subgroup \( g \) of the phase symmetries of a hypersurface \( x_m \) within a weighted projective space, specified by a quasi-homogeneous polynomial \( w_m \). The first approach, known as the Berglund-Hübsch-Krawitz (BHK) construction, involves another weighted projective space and the quotient of a new hypersurface \( x_{m^t} \) within it by a dual group \( g^t \). The second approach, proposed by Batyrev, constructs the mirror partner as a hypersurface in the toric variety defined by the reflexive polytope dual to the one associated with the original Calabi-Yau orbifold. We provide straightforward evidence supporting the equivalence of these two constructions.

Fellow TEDsters, we find ourselves navigating profoundly challenging economic waters—times that test resilience and demand innovative perspectives. Yet amidst this turbulence, I wish to share an extraordinary, if often overlooked, narrative of commercial triumph over the past two decades. This phenomenon stands as a singular parallel—in its own unconventional manner—to the meteoric rises of Microsoft or Google. Remarkably, this sector has weathered our current economic downturn with striking composure.  

I speak, of course, of organized crime.  

Now, you might rightly observe that syndicated criminal enterprises are hardly a recent development—and you’d be absolutely correct. But the past twenty years have witnessed an unparalleled surge in their scale and influence, with illicit networks now contributing an estimated 15% of global GDP. I’ve come to term this sprawling underworld the "Global Shadow Economy"—or, more succinctly, *McMafia*.  

So what catalyzed this staggering expansion of transnational crime?

Entanglement is a key resource for quantum information processing. A widely used tool for detecting entanglement is entanglement witness, which measures the witness operator and is guaranteed to be positive for separable states and can be negative for entangled states. In reality, due to the exponentially increasing Hilbert space dimension with respect to the system size, it is very challenging to construct an efficient entanglement witness for general multipartite entangled states. For $n$-partite Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ)-like states, the most robust witness scheme requires $n+1$ local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/2$ white noise. As a comparison, the most efficient witness for GHZ-like states only needs two local measurement settings and can tolerate up to $1/3$ white noise.

Currently, the majority of refugees reside in cities rather than in refugee camps, accounting for over 60 percent of the global refugee population. With this urban shift, there is an urgent need for a change in approach and new strategies. Instead of spending resources on building barriers, it would be more beneficial to invest in programs that enable refugees to support themselves. When fleeing, refugees often have to leave their possessions behind, but they retain their skills and knowledge. If given the opportunity to lead productive lives, refugees can not only support themselves but also contribute significantly to their host countries. I hail from the city of Bukavu in South Kivu, located in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and I am the fifth child in a family of 12.

Difference-in-differences analysis with a control group that differs considerably from a treated group is vulnerable to bias from historical events that have different effects on the groups. Constructing a more closely matched control group by matching a subset of the overall control group to the treated group may result in less bias. We study this phenomenon in simulation studies.

We examine the effect of mountaintop removal mining (Mrm) on mortality using a difference-in-differences analysis that makes use of the increase in Mrm following the 1990 Clean Air Act amendments. For a difference-in-differences analysis of the effect of Mrm on mortality, we constructed a more closely matched control group and found a 95% confidence interval that contains substantial adverse effects, as well as no effect and small beneficial effects.

There are hundreds of thousands of individuals on transplant waiting lists, desperately in need of life-saving organs such as kidneys, hearts, and livers. Unfortunately, the demand for these vital organs far outstrips the supply, with a severe shortage of available donor organs. What if, instead of waiting, we could create brand-new, customized organs from scratch? This is the concept behind bioprinting, a cutting-edge branch of regenerative medicine currently under development. While we are not yet able to print complex organs, we have already made significant progress in creating simpler tissues, including blood vessels and tubes responsible for facilitating nutrient and waste exchange. Bioprinting is a biological cousin of 3D printing, a technique that deposits layers of material on top of each other to construct a three-dimensional object, one layer at a time.

Emerging two-dimensional materials with low symmetry—including ReS₂ and ReSe₂ monolayers, phosphorene, group-IV monochalcogenides, and borophene—exhibit far greater structural complexity than conventional honeycomb lattices like graphene, hBN, or transition metal dichalcogenides. This diminished symmetry unlocks remarkable anisotropic phenomena: spatially varying electronic, optical, valleytronic, and spin responses, alongside emergent properties such as ferroelastic/ferroelectric behavior, magnetic ordering, spin-wave dynamics, enhanced nonlinear optics, robust photogalvanic effects, and unconventional superconductivity. Furthermore, reduced symmetry enables exotic electronic topology, nonlinear elasticity, and structural phase transitions absent in high-symmetry systems. This special issue, "Beyond Graphene: Low-Symmetry and Anisotropic 2D Materials," showcases cutting-edge advances in understanding these quantum materials through both experimental discovery and theoretical innovation.

Summer television was once a barren landscape of reruns and lackluster programming. However, this season has brought two exceptional new series that have captivated me over the past week.  

*Blow Out* presents the most ironically gay premise imaginable, yet features surprisingly few LGBTQ+ characters. The show follows celebrity hairstylist Jonathan Antin as he attempts to expand his wildly successful West Hollywood salon by opening a second location in Beverly Hills. Under normal circumstances, this venture would hardly warrant attention—least of all mine. But while channel-surfing, I caught a fleeting glimpse of Jonathan, radiating equal parts narcissism and tedium, as he stood in an empty Rodeo Drive storefront and dramatically proclaimed (in his best public voice), *"I will create the greatest salon the world has ever seen!"*  

And just like that, I was hooked. Not since *The Rocco DiSpirito Show* have I encountered such a mesmerizingly insufferable figure on television.

We introduce a novel, mixed-variable symplectic (mvs) integrator for simulating planetary systems, capable of resolving close encounters with unparalleled accuracy. Our method leverages a time-regularized approach, which maintains the stability properties of symplectic integrators while reducing the effective step size during close encounters. By employing a high-order mvs scheme, we can integrate large time steps far from close encounters, ensuring precise calculations even at vast distances. We demonstrate the algorithm's ability to resolve nearly exact collisions (i.e., with a mutual separation of a fraction of the physical radius) while utilizing the same time-step as in weakly perturbed systems, such as the solar system. In our simulations, we examine the long-term behavior of six super-Earths experiencing strong scattering forces over a period of 50,000 years. We compare our algorithm to hybrid methods, including Mercury, and show that for an equivalent computational cost, we achieve significantly better energy conservation and more accurate results.

The **direpack** package introduces a comprehensive suite of contemporary statistical dimension reduction techniques to Python, unifying them within a cohesive and user-friendly framework. It encompasses three primary methodological categories: projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust M-estimators for dimension reduction. Additionally, the package offers regularized regression estimators derived from these reduced-dimension spaces, spanning traditional principal component regression to advanced sparse partial robust M-regression.  

To enhance usability, **direpack** integrates both classical and robust preprocessing tools—such as generalized spatial signs—along with specialized visualization features and cross-validation utilities. Designed for seamless compatibility with **scikit-learn**, the package adheres to its API standards, ensuring effortless integration into statistical and machine learning pipelines. This makes **direpack** a versatile and powerful tool for modern data analysis workflows.

The attached spreadsheet (Jdasovic.nsf) details the implicit commodity charges derived from four distinct rates under the same rate schedule (Schedule DR), each with different effective dates. Notably, the rate schedule effective from August to September 2000 results in significantly higher commodity charges due to a lump sum reimbursement SDG&E customers will receive during this period. This reimbursement will lead to reduced electricity bills for customers.  

Excluding the lump sum reimbursement from the implicit energy component calculations, the average implicit commodity charge is 6.2 cents per kWh for June 2000 through January 2001, increasing to 7.3 cents for February 2001 through December 2001. The overall average across the entire period (June 2000 to December 2001) remains consistent at 6.9 cents per kWh. This average also holds for deliveries commencing September 1, 2000.

More than 2,000 business and political leaders from around the world are converging on the Swiss mountain resort of Davos for the annual World Economic Forum (WEF). For five days, they will engage in discussions on a range of pressing global issues, including China's economic prowess and Iraq's future after this Sunday's elections. Notable attendees include UK Prime Minister Tony Blair and South African President Thabo Mbeki, among more than 20 government leaders and heads of state. In a departure from previous years, the WEF is expected to be less contentious, as anti-globalization campaigners have called off a demonstration planned for the weekend. Meanwhile, the Brazilian city of Porto Alegre will host the rival World Social Forum, which will run concurrently with the WEF's more exclusive event in Davos.

I have two questions for you. One: whose shoulders do you stand on? and two: what do you stand for? these are two questions that i always begin my poetry workshops with students because at times, poetry can seem like this dead art form for old white men who just seem like they were born to be old, like, you know, benjamin button or something. And i ask my students these two questions, and then i share how i answer them, which is in these three sentences that go: i am the daughter of black writers, who are descended from freedom fighters who broke their chains and changed the world. They call me. And these are words i repeat in a mantra before every single poetry performance. In fact, i was doing it in the corner over there. I was making faces.

In the autumn of 1974, I embarked on what felt like my 13th attempt to conquer the second chapter of my high school algebra textbook. As a self-proclaimed non-math whiz, I detest mathematics. In fact, I am math-aphobic. You can imagine my frustration. As I wrestled to decipher the complexities of algebra, the doorbell rang, interrupting my struggle. I set my book aside and went to answer the door, only to find it was an empty gesture. I returned to my studies, just as I was on the cusp of grasping a glimmer of understanding, the doorbell rang once more. This time, my epiphany vanished as suddenly as it had appeared. I opened the door, only to find it was another prank, leaving me, a struggling math student, feeling bewildered and bemused.

A groundbreaking report from the University College London reveals that reducing the drink-drive limit by a third could save significantly more lives than previously thought. The study, conducted by renowned transport expert Professor Richard Allsop, suggests that decreasing the limit from 80mg of alcohol per 100ml of blood to 50mg would lead to a substantial reduction in fatalities. According to the research, this change would result in 65 lives saved annually, as well as a staggering 230 fewer serious injuries on the UK's roads, compared to 2003. This reduction in road accidents would also translate to a substantial economic benefit of £119 million per annum. Interestingly, a previous estimate from a 1998 Department of Transport document had predicted that such a move could save 50 lives annually. The findings are being published as MPs prepare to vote on the government's road safety bill, which includes changes to speeding fines but not the drink-drive limit.

We introduce a novel mixed-variable symplectic (MVS) integrator designed for planetary systems, capable of accurately resolving close encounters. This method employs a time regularization technique that preserves the stability advantages of symplectic integrators while dynamically adjusting the step size during planetary close approaches. By implementing a high-order MVS scheme, the algorithm maintains computational efficiency with large time steps in regions of weak perturbations, such as in typical solar system configurations.  

Our results demonstrate the integrator's exceptional precision, successfully handling near-collisions (with separations as small as a fraction of a planetary radius) without reducing the time step below that required for weakly perturbed systems. We validate its long-term stability by simulating a chaotic six-super-Earth system over 50,000 years, observing sustained strong scattering interactions.  

Comparative analysis against hybrid methods, such as Mercury, reveals superior energy conservation at equivalent computational cost, establishing our approach as a robust and efficient solution for high-fidelity planetary dynamics simulations.

Routing controllability of autonomous vehicles (avs) has been shown to reduce the impact of selfish routing on network efficiency. However, the assumption that avs would readily allow themselves to be controlled externally by a central agency is unrealistic. In this paper, we propose a joint routing and pricing control scheme that aims to incentivize avs to seek centrally controlled system optimal (so) routing by saving on tolls while user equilibrium (ue) seeking avs and human-driven vehicles (hvs) are subject to a congestion charge. The problem is formulated as a bi-level optimization, in which dynamic tolls are optimized in the upper level, whereas the lower level is a mixed equilibrium simulation-based dynamic traffic assignment model considering mixed fleet of avs and hvs.

This paper presents a millimeter-scale cmos 64$\times$64 single charged particle radiation detector system for external beam cancer radiotherapy. A 1$\times$1 $\mu m^2$ diode measures energy deposition by a single charged particle in the depletion region, and the array design provides a large detection area of 512$\times$512 $\mu m^2$. Instead of sensing the voltage drop caused by radiation, the proposed system measures the pulse width, i.e., the time it takes for the voltage to return to its baseline. This obviates the need for using power-hungry and large analog-to-digital converters. A prototype asic is fabricated in tsmc 65 nm lp cmos process and consumes the average static power of 0.535 mw under 1.2 v analog and digital power supply. The functionality of the whole system is successfully verified in a clinical 67.5 mev proton beam setting.

Manchester United's scrappy victory over Manchester City reduced Chelsea's Premier League lead to nine points after a thrilling encounter. Wayne Rooney's cross to the near post, met by Gary Neville, was met with a low shot that deflected off Richard Dunne, putting United ahead. Seven minutes later, the unfortunate Dunne's volley over David James' head and into his own net added to United's woes. Steve McManaman squandered City's best chance when he shot wide from just three yards out in the first half. In the opening 45 minutes, United's approach play was more laboured than patient, and they managed to fashion just one chance - a Paul Scholes header over the bar - in a first half that saw them struggle to create clear-cut opportunities. Their inability to maintain possession and create clear-cut chances left them unlikely to earn the win they needed to maintain any chance of catching Chelsea in the title race.

Iraq to invite phone licence bids iraq is to invite bids for two telephone licences, saying it wants to significantly boost nationwide coverage over the next decade. Bids have been invited from local, arab and foreign companies, iraq's ministry of communications said. The winner will work in partnership with the iraqi telecommunications and post company (itpc). The firms will install and operate a fixed phone network, providing voice, fax and internet services. The ministry said that it wanted to increase iraq's "very low telephone service penetration rate from about 4.5% today to about 25% within 10 years." it also hopes to develop a "highly visible and changeable telecommunication sector". Details of the bidding and tender process will be published on the ministry's website on 9 february. It also is planning a road-show for investors in amman, jordan.

In a significant blow to the spamming industry, the self-proclaimed "Spam King" of the internet has been forced to seek bankruptcy protection. Scott Richter, the mastermind behind the notorious spamming operation OptinRealBig.com, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy due to the mounting pressure of numerous lawsuits. The company, which has been embroiled in several high-profile legal battles, is most notably facing a multi-million dollar damages claim from Microsoft. By filing for Chapter 11, OptinRealBig aims to restructure its operations and resolve its outstanding legal issues while continuing to trade. As the third-largest spammer in the world, according to Spamhaus, a leading anti-spam watchdog, OptinRealBig has been at the center of a long-standing controversy. In December 2003, the company was sued by Microsoft and New York Attorney General Eliot Spitzer for violating anti-spam laws, specifically for sending billions of unsolicited emails through 514 compromised IP addresses in 35 countries.

Estimating the intrinsic dimension of a dataset is a crucial step in many dimensionality reduction techniques. This article introduces "intrinsic," an R package featuring advanced, state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators for determining a dataset's intrinsic dimension. The package includes methods such as the two-nn, gride, and hidalgo models. To enhance accessibility, it provides user-friendly, high-level functions built on a robust set of efficient, low-level routines. The intrinsic package offers models in two categories: homogeneous and heterogeneous intrinsic dimension estimators. The first category includes the two-nn and gride models, with functions that support inference in both frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. In the second category is the hidalgo model, a Bayesian mixture model, which incorporates an efficient Gibbs sampler for implementation.

We study the phenomenology of a hypercharge-zero su(2) triplet scalar whose existence is motivated by two-step electroweak symmetry-breaking. We consider both the possibility that the triplets are stable and contribute to the dark matter density, or that they decay via mixing with the standard model higgs boson. The former is constrained by disappearing charged track searches at the lhc and by dark matter direct detection experiments, while the latter is constrained by existing multilepton collider searches. We find that a two-step electroweak phase transition involving a stable triplet with a negative quadratic term is ruled out by direct detection searches, while an unstable triplet with a mass less than $230\ \mathrm{gev}$ is excluded at $95\%$ confidence level.

She had everything—saucers, teapots, coffee cups, wine goblets, water glasses, and seven different kinds of forks and spoons, not to mention dinner plates, salad plates, dessert plates, soup bowls, and side bowls. Last night in class, we explored this week's assigned reading: Edward S. Casey's "Getting Back into Place: Toward a Renewed Study of the Place-World." The first chapter, "Implacement," and the second, "Displacement," delved into how our modern world emphasizes time as the key framework: we are constantly racing against time, trying to save it, or catching up just in time. Casey argues that centering culture around place fosters a more rooted community. (This is a brief summary, offered humbly in case my professor or any classmates read this post.) So far, this book is the first in our course that has truly captivated my interest.

British stars denied major oscars british hopes of winning major oscars were dashed as uk stars failed to win acting and directing prizes. Despite three nominations, mike leigh's vera drake failed to take the director or screenplay awards and there was no prize for star imelda staunton. Kate winslet also lost to best actress hilary swank, while clive owen and sophie okonedo failed to win best supporting actor awards. The uk's andrea arnold won the short film award for her gritty drama wasp. Arnold, who presented children's television shows number 73 and motormouth in the 1980s, said it was "totally overwhelming" to win. Wasp tells the story of a single mother living on the breadline, beating films by fellow uk nominees gary mckendry and ashvin kumar.

This game turned out to be an unexpected treasure. Although rave reviews can sometimes exaggerate a product's appeal, in this instance, they truly capture the enjoyment this delightful little game offers. It uniquely combines elements of a classic 2D platformer with those of a MOBA, pulling it off with flair. To my surprise, I enjoyed playing characters that I'd typically overlook in other MOBA games. Initially, I thought this might not extend to all character types, but I decided to try a melee tank nonetheless. I was pleasantly surprised by the experience, which motivated me to explore more characters as I unlocked them through gameplay. The art style and interface possess a peculiar charm that captivated me. I even adopted one of the characters as my avatar and chose a game-related wallpaper after creating a card set.

As I begin my poetry workshops, I pose two fundamental questions to my students  'Whose shoulders do you stand on?' and 'What do you stand for?' These inquiries are crucial because, at times, poetry can seem like a seemingly inaccessible art form, often associated with an elitist past, as embodied by figures like Benjamin Franklin or other historical literary giants. However, I share my own response to these questions, which is a powerful declaration that I repeat before every performance  'I am the daughter of black writers, descended from freedom fighters who broke their chains and reshaped the world.' These words serve as a personal mantra, a reminder of my heritage and the responsibility that comes with it. In fact, I was rehearsing them in the corner over there, making sure to embody the power and conviction behind them.

This game has been an absolute delight, despite a few notable imperfections. The absence of a more robust save system and the ever-present threat of sudden death can be infuriating at times. My first playthrough was marred by the loss of my main character around the 10-hour mark, which was roughly halfway through the game. The inability to reload a previous save at that critical juncture was particularly frustrating. To mitigate such catastrophic failures, I've since resorted to utilizing a third-party mod and save tool. Additionally, the aiming reticule could benefit from being slightly larger, and the zoom feature feels somewhat underwhelming. Nevertheless, the game's unique blend of strategy, mission-based gameplay, and first-person shooter and beat-em-up elements has proven to be an irresistible combination. As someone who doesn't typically gravitate towards zombie-themed games, I've found myself thoroughly enthralled by this experience. The game's engaging nature has led me to play it whenever possible, often finding myself drawn into just one more mission or supply run, and frequently having to tear myself away to ensure a safe exit before shutting down the game.

**Ahimsa: The Timeless Principle of Nonviolence**  

*Ahimsa* (Sanskrit: अहिंसा, pronounced *uh-him-sah*) is the ethical doctrine of refraining from harm to all living beings. Derived from the Sanskrit roots *a-* (not) and *himsa* (injury), it embodies compassion in thought, word, and deed.  

As Mahatma Gandhi reflected in *The Story of My Experiments with Truth* (1927), his deepening understanding of *ahimsa* led him to scrutinize even his innermost thoughts and expressions. He found lines like *"Scatter her enemies and make them fall; confound their policies, frustrate their knavish tricks"* deeply at odds with his commitment to nonviolence.  

Born on October 2, 1869, Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi may seem a figure of a distant past, yet his teachings on peace, justice, and *ahimsa* remain profoundly relevant in our modern world. His legacy endures as a guiding light for humanity.

Deadline nears for fiat-gm deal fiat and general motors (gm) have until midnight on 1 february to settle a disagreement over a potential takeover. The deadline marks the point at which fiat will gain the right to sell its car division to gm, part of an alliance agreed in 2000. Gm, whose own european operations are losing money, no longer wants to own the unprofitable fiat unit. Reports of deadlocked talks sent fiat shares down 1.2% on tuesday, after monday's 4% gain on hopes of a payoff. The us firm is thought to be offering about $2bn (£1.06bn) to extricate itself from the arrangement. It has argued the deal was voided by fiat's decision to sell off fiat's finance arm and halve gm's stake via a capital-raising effort. The 2000 deal resulted from a race between gm and daimlerchrysler to ally with fiat.

This past weekend was truly enjoyable. I indulged in nature's finest greenery, listened to Nevermore—a band I wish I had explored much earlier—and spent much of my time reading. I managed to finish three books: Bruce Sterling's "Heavy Weather," Larry Niven's "World of Ptavvs," and Octavia Butler's "Parable of the Sower." "Heavy Weather" offered an engaging cyberpunk journey through a near-future United States plagued by global warming and chaos. The book was clever, well-paced, and convincing. However, I must admit that I didn't come away with a strong impression of the story or its characters. While both are well-developed and vividly described, the book struck me as good, but not exceptional.

DOVES SOAR TO UK ALBUM SUMMIT WITH MANCHESTER ROCK BAND'S NEW RELEASE

Doves have reached the number one spot on the UK album chart with their latest release, "Some Cities." The Manchester rock band replaces flamboyant US act Scissor Sisters at the top of the chart. The album follows the successful single "Black and White Town," which reached number six. R&B star Nelly has the new number one single with "Over and Over," a collaboration with Tim McGraw. Girls Aloud, Akon, and Kaiser Chiefs all have new singles in the top ten, as do Futureheads and Usher. Elvis Presley's latest re-release, "(Marie's the Name)," his latest flame, entered the chart at number three, one place ahead of Girls Aloud's "Wake Me Up." Hip-hop performer Akon's "Locked Up" is at number five, while hotly-tipped Leeds band Kaiser Chiefs have their second chart hit at number six with "Oh My God.

Extreme mass ratio inspirals (emris) can be classified as dry emris and wet emris based on their formation mechanisms. Dry (or the "loss-cone") emris, previsouly considered as the main emri sources for the laser interferometer space antenna, are primarily produced by multi-body scattering in the nuclear star cluster and gravitational capture. In this letter, we highlight an alternative emri formation channel: (wet) emri formation assisted by the accretion flow around accreting galactic-center massive black holes (mbhs). In this channel, the accretion disk captures stellar-mass black holes that are intially moving on inclined orbits, and subsequently drives them to migrate towards the mbh - this process boosts the formation rate of emris in such galaxies by orders of magnitude.

Iraq is inviting bids from local, Arab, and foreign companies to bid for two telephone licenses, with the goal of significantly increasing the country's telephone coverage over the next decade. The Ministry of Communications has invited bids from companies to install and operate a fixed phone network, providing voice, fax, and internet services. The ministry aims to increase Iraq's "very low telephone service penetration rate" of around 4.5% today to around 25% within the next 10 years. Additionally, the ministry hopes to develop a "highly visible and changeable telecommunications sector." The bidding and tender process will be published on the ministry's website on February 9, and the ministry is also planning a roadshow for investors in Amman, Jordan.

When developing models to summarize clinical data for simulations, it is good practice to evaluate their ability to accurately reproduce the data. This can be achieved by means of visual predictive checks (VPC), which involve (1) multiple replications of the original study by simulation from the model under evaluation, (2) calculating estimates of interest for each simulated study and (3) comparing the distribution of those estimates with the estimate from the original study. This approach is a straightforward method that can be applied in most cases, including the analysis of time-to-event data and considering the special case where a time-varying covariate is unknown or cannot be approximated after event time.

The revival of the Devil May Cry franchise has ushered in a transformative era for the series, with its iconic protagonist, Dante, undergoing a significant metamorphosis. Although die-hard fans of the original series may initially lament the changes to their cherished character, this bold new direction, as exemplified by Ninja Theory's latest installment, is undoubtedly a step in the right direction. DmC serves as an ideal entry point for newcomers to the series, offering a captivating origin story that delves into the complexities of a once-arrogant and cynical Dante, and chronicles his poignant journey towards self-discovery and altruism. Through a masterfully woven narrative arc, the game breathes new life into the character, rendering him a compelling and relatable figure to behold. As the story unfolds, Dante is thrust into a corrupt world he had previously chosen to ignore, marking the beginning of a transformative odyssey that will challenge his perceptions and test his resolve.

When I was 14, my family was in the process of adopting my younger brothers from Ethiopia. One day, my mom posed an unexpected question: "What date should we choose for their birthdays?" I replied in disbelief, "The day they were born, of course!" To which my mom responded, "Kristen, neither of your little brothers has a birth certificate, so how do you suggest we find out their actual birthdays?" This revelation was eye-opening for me. Fast forward 20 years, and I am still deeply engaged with this issue. However, instead of solely focusing on my brothers' lack of birth certificates, I am now working on finding solutions to this problem on a global scale. But you might wonder, what is the connection between birth certificates and international development?

The Weak Gravity Conjecture (WGC) posits that in any consistent quantum theory of gravity, there must exist superextremal particles. Conventionally, these particles are thought to ensure that extremal black holes are either unstable or only marginally stable. However, the consequences of this not being the case are not entirely clear. This note demonstrates that in a general Einstein quantum theory of gravity within Anti-de Sitter (AdS) space, the precise stability of extremal black branes conflicts with established quantum information theorems concerning entanglement entropy. To resolve this contradiction, the note introduces a nonperturbative version of the WGC, which aligns with the traditional interpretation in the context of weak coupling. This argument is broad, requiring neither supersymmetry nor a specific ultraviolet (UV) completion. It relies solely on the principles of Einstein gravity, effective field theory, and holography.

According to Chinese state media, the China Three Gorges Project Corporation is defying a government directive to halt the construction of one of its massive dams. Despite orders, the company responsible for building the Three Gorges Dam is proceeding with the construction of the Xiluodu Dam, as reported by the Beijing News. The Xiluodu Dam is among 30 large-scale projects ordered to stop due to insufficient environmental assessments. Instead of complying, the company may choose to pay fines, as suggested by the Beijing News. Additionally, the firm has disregarded instructions to cease work on two other projects: the Three Gorges Underground Power Plant and the Three Gorges Project Electrical Power Supply Plant. To date, only 22 out of the 30 projects targeted by China's State Environmental Protection Agency (SEPA) for failing to perform mandatory environmental impact assessments have adhered to the suspension order.

Within general relativity, the unique stationary solution of an isolated black hole is the kerr spacetime, which has a peculiar multipolar structure depending only on its mass and spin. We develop a general method to extract the multipole moments of arbitrary stationary spacetimes and apply it to a large family of horizonless microstate geometries. The latter can break the axial and equatorial symmetry of the kerr metric and have a much richer multipolar structure, which provides a portal to constrain fuzzball models phenomenologically. We find numerical evidence that all multipole moments are typically larger (in absolute value) than those of a kerr black hole with the same mass and spin. Current measurements of the quadrupole moment of black-hole candidates could place only mild constraints on fuzzballs, while future gravitational-wave detections of extreme mass-ratio inspirals with the space mission lisa will improve these bounds by orders of magnitude.

Roxy music on isle of wight bill roxy music will appear at june's isle of wight music festival, along with morrissey, supergrass and idlewild. Rem have already been confirmed as headliners for the three-day event, which takes place from 10-12 june. Original band members bryan ferry, phil manzerana, andy mackay and paul thompson will take to the stage on the saturday night of the festival. The band are also working in the studio on new material, their first since the 1982 album avalon. Roxy music were formed in 1971 by ferry, mackay and brian eno, with manzanera joining the following year. Their first hit was 1972's virginia plain, with other hits including street life, love is the drug and the 1981 john lennon cover, jealous guy.

The determination of a dataset's intrinsic dimension is a crucial step in various dimensionality reduction techniques. This article showcases Intrinsic, an innovative R package that incorporates state-of-the-art likelihood-based estimators for assessing a dataset's intrinsic dimension. The package includes three cutting-edge models: two-nn, gride, and Hidalgo. These models are designed to be user-friendly and efficient, with a focus on both frequentist and Bayesian inference. Intrinsic encompasses two categories of intrinsic dimension estimators: homogeneous and heterogeneous. The former includes the two-nn and gride models, which provide flexible inference options under both frequentist and Bayesian frameworks. The latter category houses Hidalgo, a Bayesian mixture model that benefits from an efficient Gibbs sampler implementation. By leveraging these advanced techniques, Intrinsic enables researchers to effortlessly access novel estimators for intrinsic dimension, streamlining the analysis process and unlocking new insights into complex datasets.

The Eastern position will be located in Ann Arbor, Michigan, and the Western position will be based in Denver, Colorado. Both Directors of Sales will report to the Vice President of Sales. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of 5 years of sales experience and an additional minimum of 2 years of experience with a trading company is preferred. The successful candidate will have a strong understanding of the U.S. coal, transportation, and electric utility industries, including coal tolling, marketing, and origination functions used in support of coal trading operations.

Jdasovic.nsf the spreadsheet below gives the implicit commodity based on the effective date of four different rates for the same rate schedule (schedule dr). The rate schedule effective august 2000 through september 2000 yields a much larger commodity charge because sdg&e customers will receive a lump sum reimbursement from sdg&e over those two months. Sdg&e customers will expect lower electric bills. Assuming that the lump sum reimbursement is not included in the implicit energy component, the average for june 2000 through january 2001 is 6.2 cts and the implicit commodity average from february 2001 through december 2001 is 7.3 cts. The average implicit commodity from june 2000 through december 2001 is 6.9 cts for deliveries starting september 1, 2000, the average would still be 6.9 cts.

In the digital marketplace, online reviews serve as a crucial resource for consumers seeking informed purchasing decisions. However, the integrity of these evaluations is often compromised by the proliferation of fake reviews, also known as "spams," orchestrated by sophisticated spammers who continually adapt and refine their tactics to evade detection. The ephemeral nature of these spamming strategies, which can shift rapidly over time, vary across spammers and targeted products, and often remain unknown, poses significant challenges to their capture. Moreover, traditional detection methods tend to prioritize accuracy over the ultimate goal of safeguarding the trustworthiness of product evaluations. To overcome these hurdles, we propose a novel approach based on a minimax game framework, where spammers and spam detectors engage in a competitive dynamic, driven by practical objectives that transcend mere detection accuracy. The Nash equilibria emerging from this game yield robust detectors that are resilient to diverse detection strategies, thereby ensuring the long-term reliability of online reviews.

The direpack package aims to establish a comprehensive set of modern statistical dimension reduction techniques within the Python universe as a unified, consistent package. The dimension reduction methods encompass three categories  projection pursuit-based dimension reduction, sufficient dimension reduction, and robust M-estimators for dimension reduction. As a corollary, regularized regression estimators based on these reduced-dimensional spaces are provided, ranging from classical principal component regression to sparse partial robust M-regression. The package also includes a comprehensive set of classical and robust pre-processing utilities, including generalized spatial signs, as well as dedicated plotting functionality and cross-validation utilities. Furthermore, direpack has been designed to seamlessly integrate with the scikit-learn API, ensuring that the estimators can be effortlessly incorporated into (statistical and/or machine) learning pipelines within that framework, thereby fostering a seamless workflow.
